description_html:
alkemy is a multi-agent AI system that lets a single consulting partner deeply steward 15–20 key accounts instead of 3–5 — by autonomously running the precipitation chain (signal → person → hook) across each account, detecting burning-platform moments, and generating partner-grade meeting prep — and we're using Agent Simulation, Observability, and Optimizer to harden it against the conflicting-signal edge cases that break single-agent systems.*


The Business Use Case


Consulting is a $500B+ industry built on partner relationships. We interviewed 23+ senior partners across Roland Berger, Oliver Wyman, and boutique firms over the past 8 months. The universal pattern:


  • Partners deeply steward 3–5 key accounts at a time because the cognitive load of tracking signals, people, and pitch contexts saturates their attention.
  • With the right instrumentation, the same partner could steward 15–20 accounts
  • Every AI tool on the market today is an analyst tool (research, market maps, decks). ChatGPT is commoditizing that layer.
  • The partner layer does not exist as a product. Signals that matter this week. Prep for Tuesday's buyer meeting. The burning-platform moment in an account you already know. No product here.


The Multi-Agent Architecture


alkemy's Key Account Agent is a collaboration of six specialized agents orchestrated via the Agent Development Kit (ADK):


1. Signal Agent — monitors external sources (news, earnings, SEC filings, LinkedIn, specialized trade press) for events affecting watched accounts.

2. Interaction Agent — ingests the partner's internal context (uploaded call recordings, meeting notes, CRM exports) via MCP connectors to Fathom / Granola / Gong / Salesforce / Dynamics.

3. Burning-Platform Synthesizer — detects the four-ingredient transformation-moment signature: (a) change/urgency at the client, (b) a problem the consultant can name, (c) external evidence of competitors moving ahead, (d) internal evidence the client can't solve alone.

4. VP-Prep Packet Agent — generates the pre-meeting brief with buyer bio, career history, company's stated strategy, new-CEO/CFO signals, and burning issues pulled from analyst calls.

5. Hero-Moment Agent — drafts the pitch framing that makes the buyer look like a hero to their boss.

6. Orchestrator — prioritizes across accounts, routes tasks, surfaces the next-best-action to the partner each morning.


All agents ground their outputs in cited evidence via Vertex AI Search over public sources + the firm's private IP. Nothing is surfaced without a source link.

description_html:
Problem to solve

Software teams and startups often lose time between idea and execution. A client shares requirements in text or PDF form, but turning that into a production-ready application still needs many manual steps: understanding the scope, choosing the architecture, selecting the stack, planning environments, writing boilerplate, setting up CI/CD, preparing deployment, and coordinating decisions across development, DevOps, and QA. This slows delivery, creates inconsistency, and makes it harder for smaller teams to move fast with confidence.

Our solution

SmartDev Agent is an AI-powered software delivery assistant that acts like a senior architect and technical co-pilot. It accepts a requirement as plain text or PDF, analyzes the project type and complexity, makes justified technical decisions, asks only the essential developer questions, and then scaffolds a production-oriented project flow end to end.

The system includes:

  • requirement parsing for text and PDF inputs
  • intelligent project classification and architecture decision-making
  • guided developer questions for GitHub setup, environment variables, and hosting
  • automatic generation of backend/frontend starter code, Docker setup, environment files, tests, and CI/CD workflows
  • MCP-style integrations for file handling, GitHub planning, cloud deployment planning, and notifications
  • both a CLI interface and a Streamlit dashboard for a smooth developer experience

Our goal is to reduce the gap between an idea and a deployable application. Instead of just generating code, SmartDev Agent helps structure the whole delivery workflow so developers can move from requirement to implementation much faster and with better technical consistency.

description_full: The Complex Business ProblemCurrent computer vision fitness tools rely on ancient models that flatten time and conflate root causes with downstream symptoms. A standard model can detect a symptom like "knee valgus," but it cannot tell a trainer if that valgus was caused by restricted ankle dorsiflexion or weak hip abductors. This creates a massive ceiling on AI utility in the B2B physical therapy and elite coaching markets. Detecting a symptom is a parlor trick; diagnosing the root cause is a highly monetizable clinical service. Elite trainers and studios need a way to scale their specific, gold-standard diagnostic reasoning across hundreds of clients without dilution. The Solution: A Per-Joint Specialist SwarmKinetic Swarm is net-new autonomous system using the the Agent Development Kit to break this problem apart. Instead of relying on a single black-box model to look at the whole body, we will build a "Kinetic Chain Swarm". We deploy parallel, isolated agents. Each agent is responsible for a single joint in the kinetic chain. Operating on declarative biomechanical ontologies engineered into their system prompts, these agents act as highly focused specialists. A coordinator Agent then arbitrates their parallel outputs to resolve the temporal sequence of a movement fault. If the Ankle Agent flags a range-of-motion limit at frame 138, and the Knee Agent flags valgus at frame 142, the Coordinator definitively diagnoses the ankle as the casual driver. Moving the Needle for Kinetic SwarmThis architecture solves the core defensibility problem in the $40Billion movement health market. By separating the pattern recognition (weights) from the clinical knowledge (YAML ontologies acting as structured prompts), we allow gyms, physical therapists, and elite trainers to audit, edit, and scale their exact coaching standards. It transforms subjective movement assessment into deterministic, liability-reducing documentation.

Drop in a job requirement and watch 5 specialized AI agents autonomously find candidates, draft personalized outreach, screen responses, and prepare submission packages — turning days of recruiter work into minutes.Problem to solveRecruiters spend 80% of their time on repetitive manual tasks — searching for candidates across LinkedIn, job boards, and databases, writing outreach emails one by one, screening responses, and preparingsubmission documents. What takes a recruiter days, RecruitAgent completes in minutes.

description_full: Problem to solveAquaculture operations are managed through manual monitoring and fixed decision rules. Operators watch sensor readings, respond to alerts, and adjust feeding or aeration based on experience and intuition. There is no system that can model farm conditions before changes are made, maintain a structured and queryable record of what is happening, or make autonomous decisions grounded in both historical state and simulated outcomes.The result is that interventions happen after problems have already surfaced. There is no way to test a change before applying it to a live system. Every decision is made in isolation, with no unified loop connecting simulation, state tracking, and automated control.Our solutionTrident is an AI agent system that manages aquaculture operations through three connected components working as a single control loop.Trident-Sandbox is the simulation environment. It models farm conditions including water quality, dissolved oxygen, ammonia buildup, fish biomass, feeding schedules, and equipment behavior. Before any action is taken, Sandbox can model what happens next under different interventions — giving the system a way to test decisions without touching the live farm.Trident-Archive is the system record. It maintains a structured, timestamped timeline of sensor readings, simulated readings, feeding events, operator actions, AI actions, alerts, and outcomes. Archive is the source of truth that both Sandbox and Engine read from and write to.Trident-Engine is the AI agent. It reads current and historical state from Archive, identifies when the system is drifting from expected behavior, models possible interventions in Sandbox, and executes the action most likely to restore stable conditions. It then writes the result back into Archive so the system can compare prediction against outcome over time.Because the control loop — read state, model outcomes, act, record results — runs the same way in simulation and in production, Trident can be tested, validated, and improved without risk to the live farm.
description_html:
Problem to solve


Aquaculture operations are managed through manual monitoring and fixed decision rules. Operators watch sensor readings, respond to alerts, and adjust feeding or aeration based on experience and intuition. There is no system that can model farm conditions before changes are made, maintain a structured and queryable record of what is happening, or make autonomous decisions grounded in both historical state and simulated outcomes.


The result is that interventions happen after problems have already surfaced. There is no way to test a change before applying it to a live system. Every decision is made in isolation, with no unified loop connecting simulation, state tracking, and automated control.


Our solution


Trident is an AI agent system that manages aquaculture operations through three connected components working as a single control loop.


Trident-Sandbox is the simulation environment. It models farm conditions including water quality, dissolved oxygen, ammonia buildup, fish biomass, feeding schedules, and equipment behavior. Before any action is taken, Sandbox can model what happens next under different interventions — giving the system a way to test decisions without touching the live farm.


Trident-Archive is the system record. It maintains a structured, timestamped timeline of sensor readings, simulated readings, feeding events, operator actions, AI actions, alerts, and outcomes. Archive is the source of truth that both Sandbox and Engine read from and write to.


Trident-Engine is the AI agent. It reads current and historical state from Archive, identifies when the system is drifting from expected behavior, models possible interventions in Sandbox, and executes the action most likely to restore stable conditions. It then writes the result back into Archive so the system can compare prediction against outcome over time.


Because the control loop — read state, model outcomes, act, record results — runs the same way in simulation and in production, Trident can be tested, validated, and improved without risk to the live farm.

Problem to solve


Africa's e-commerce market is one of the fastest-growing in the world, projected to reach $113 billion by 2029, growing at nearly 12% annually. Kenya alone is the continent's third-largest e-commerce market, with the sector expected to grow from $2.3 billion in 2024 to $3.8 billion by 2028. Over 60% of these transactions happen on mobile devices, and commerce increasingly lives on WhatsApp and Instagram.


Yet the SMEs driving this growth are operating with no infrastructure. They receive 100–300 customer inquiries daily on WhatsApp and Instagram but cannot respond fast enough, losing sales to faster competitors. After a sale, operations collapse, orders get lost, stock is oversold, deliveries go untracked, and owners make restocking decisions with zero data. These businesses are losing revenue at every step of the customer journey, not because they lack customers, but because they lack the infrastructure to serve them.


Our solution

Operator is a multi-agent AI system that automates the entire revenue operation of African e-commerce SMEs. Built on Google ADK with four specialized agents:

Sofia (Sales Agent) handles all customer conversations on WhatsApp and Instagram — qualifying leads, closing sales, and processing M-Pesa payments in under 30 seconds, in English and Swahili.

Gilfoyle (Inventory Agent) manages stock in real time, preventing overselling and alerting owners before items run out.

Jared (Fulfilment Agent) coordinates order dispatch and proactively updates customers on delivery status. Richard (Intelligence Agent) ingests data from all agents and delivers weekly plain-language business intelligence to the owner on WhatsApp. Together, the four agents run the complete revenue operation — autonomously.

description_html:
Problem to Solve

In the post-COVID era, the global e-commerce landscape has undergone a massive transformation, evolving from a convenience channel into a dominant force in retail. By 2026, global e-commerce sales are expected to reach $7.4 trillion, contributing over 22% of total retail sales worldwide.

India stands out as one of the fastest-growing markets, projected to reach $160–226 billion by 2026. This growth is fueled by rapid smartphone adoption, low-cost internet, UPI-based payments, and a young, digitally native population. A major disruptor within this ecosystem is quick commerce (q-commerce), expected to reach nearly $7 billion, with platforms promising delivery within 10–30 minutes.


Core Problem: Lack of a Single Source of Truth (SSOT)

As brands expand across multiple channels—marketplaces, quick commerce platforms, D2C websites, and social commerce—their data ecosystem becomes increasingly fragmented.

Each platform operates independently with its own:

  • Data structures and formats
  • APIs and reporting systems
  • Update frequencies (real-time vs delayed)
  • Metrics definitions

This creates a scenario where critical business data—orders, inventory, ads, returns, pricing, logistics, and payouts—is scattered across disconnected systems.

To manage this complexity, companies build multiple ERP systems, dashboards, and spreadsheets. They also hire teams of analysts, SQL developers, and data engineers whose primary responsibility often becomes data reconciliation rather than value creation.


Key Challenges Faced by Businesses

  • Inventory Inefficiencies: Lack of synchronization leads to overselling (causing cancellations and refunds) or stockouts (lost revenue and poor customer experience).
  • Delayed Decision-Making: Data aggregation delays prevent real-time pricing, promotion, and demand optimization.
  • High Operational Costs: Significant resources are spent on manual data cleaning and reporting.
  • No Unified Business View: Absence of a 360° view across channels limits strategic planning and personalization.
  • Scalability Issues: During peak demand (e.g., festive sales or quick commerce spikes), manual processes fail to keep up.

Industry estimates suggest that businesses lose 5–15% of potential revenue due to these inefficiencies. In quick commerce, where speed is critical, even minor delays in data can result in poor inventory allocation and missed hyperlocal demand.


Why Existing Solutions Fall Short

Traditional ERP and analytics solutions attempt to solve this problem but introduce new limitations:

  • High implementation and maintenance costs
  • Rigid architectures that struggle with new integrations
  • Slow adaptation to frequent API and platform changes
  • Continued dependence on technical teams

This leads to a fragmented, expensive, and inefficient ecosystem—especially challenging for SMBs that lack large technical resources.


Our Solution: UniSync AI

UniSync AI is an intelligent, cloud-native platform designed to act as both a Single Source of Truth and a personal AI analytics team for e-commerce businesses.

Instead of relying on multiple analysts and engineers, UniSync AI uses a multi-agent AI architecture powered by models from Google to automate data ingestion, standardization, analysis, and decision-making.


Multi-Agent Architecture

  • Supervisor Agent: Acts as the central brain, interpreting user queries, breaking them into tasks, coordinating agents, and delivering unified insights.
  • Order Agent: Monitors order lifecycle, cancellations, returns, and fulfillment performance across platforms in real time.
  • Inventory Agent: Maintains unified inventory visibility, predicts stockouts, prevents overselling, and optimizes replenishment—critical for quick commerce.
  • Ads Agent: Evaluates campaign performance, tracks ROI, and recommends budget allocation and bidding strategies.
  • PnL Agent: Builds real-time profitability views by combining revenue, ad spend, fees, logistics, and returns.
  • RCA Agent: Performs root cause analysis for anomalies such as sales drops or high cancellations, providing clear explanations backed by data.


How It Works

Data from multiple platforms is continuously ingested and standardized into a unified layer built on DB, forming a reliable SSOT. AI models are powered by Gemini process this data in real time.


The Supervisor Agent orchestrates responses across agents and delivers clear, actionable insights instantly.


Key Benefits

  • Eliminates dependency on large data and analytics teams
  • Provides real-time, accurate, and unified insights
  • Enables faster and smarter decision-making
  • Reduces operational costs significantly
  • Scales efficiently for both SMBs and enterprises


Conclusion

UniSync AI transforms fragmented, multi-channel e-commerce data into a centralized intelligence system. By replacing manual processes with autonomous AI agents, it empowers businesses to operate efficiently, improve profitability, and compete effectively in the rapidly evolving digital commerce ecosystem.

description_html:
Ready to start building? Check out this guide for next steps.

Problem to solve


Road infrastructure deteriorates over time due to traffic load, weather conditions, and poor maintenance. This leads to multiple types of road distress such as potholes, cracks, surface wear, edge failures, and uneven pavements.


Today, most road inspections are manual teams physically survey roads, which is time-consuming, expensive, and often inconsistent. As a result:

  • Damage is detected late
  • Severity is not accurately assessed
  • Maintenance decisions are delayed

This directly impacts public safety, increases vehicle damage, and raises long-term repair costs. There is a clear need for a faster, more reliable, and scalable way to monitor road conditions.


Our solution


We are building an AI-powered Road Intelligence Agent that automates road condition monitoring and analysis.


The system uses computer vision and intelligent agents to:

  • Detect multiple types of road distress (potholes, cracks, surface degradation, uneven roads) using a YOLOv8 model
  • Analyze the severity of each issue (low, medium, high) based on visual patterns
  • Provide actionable recommendations for maintenance (e.g., patching, resurfacing, urgent repair)
  • Generate structured reports that can assist authorities in prioritizing repairs

By combining machine learning with an agent-based approach, the system not only identifies problems but also interprets them and suggests next steps. This makes road maintenance more proactive, efficient, and scalable.

description_full: RealtyOps OS is an AI operating system for real-estate brokerage teams that turns inbound leads into scheduled property tours with speed, consistency, and trust. Most broker teams lose revenue because follow-up is manual, inconsistent, and delayed across channels. Leads come from website forms and ad campaigns, but response quality depends on agent availability. RealtyOps OS solves this by running a hybrid-autonomous workflow: it qualifies leads, initiates personalized outreach, handles follow-up cadence, negotiates available slots, syncs with HubSpot and Google Calendar, and books tours automatically when qualification criteria are met. For ambiguous or high-risk cases, it routes decisions to a human broker with full context. Built with Gemini and Google’s agent tooling, RealtyOps OS is designed as a channel-flexible system architecture rather than a single chatbot. In this version, it supports website and Meta lead intake, with extensible connectors for additional sources. The system enforces clear qualification gates (budget range, preferred locality, move-in timeline, verified contact), executes a 3-step follow-up cadence, and maintains an auditable action trail for operational trust. Our core business goal is measurable revenue impact. RealtyOps OS is benchmarked with an A/B simulation baseline and targets: - +15% tour-scheduled conversion rate - +25% follow-up completion rate - Under 3 minutes median lead response time RealtyOps OS helps broker teams move from ad spend to booked tours faster, while giving founders and sales leaders a reliable AI execution layer they can actually trust in production

description_html:
Problem to Solve

Multi-location small businesses and franchises in Latin America (convenience stores, hardware networks, grocery franchisees) are currently losing significant margins due to three critical friction points:

  • The "Knowledge Gap" & High Turnover: Training a 19-year-old on their first job to master complex POS software, memorize thousands of stock prices, and understand business rules takes up to week of paid onboarding; 2 months later the employee leaves. In a high-turnover environment, this cost is a constant, unproductive drain on capital.
  • Operational Chaos (The "Paper & WhatsApp" Trap): Cashiers currently juggle paper notebooks, informal WhatsApp pings to owners, and long Excel sheets to reconstruct daily cash flow. This disorganized workflow is highly prone to human error, creates trust gaps between owners and staff, and leaves headquarters with zero real-time visibility across stores.
  • Prohibitive Scaling Costs: Traditional retail tech requires legacy point-of-sale (POS) hardware ($500–$1,500 per checkout) and per-seat software licenses. For a growing franchise, these capital expenditures (CAPEX) make scaling slow and financially risky.

The result: Inventory drifts until it runs out mid-sale, owners can't trust their numbers, and businesses remain "blind" to their own operations.

Our Solution

Velora is a B2B AI-native operating system for multi-location businesses, built 100% on the Google Cloud ecosystem. We replace legacy POS hardware with an intelligent multi-agent architecture that slashes training from weeks to minutes, and the employees cellphone becomes the buisiness terminal, saving costs by a big margin.


The Architecture: Google-Native Intelligence

  • Employee Agent (Front-Line Ops): Built with Gemini 2.5 Flash and orchestrated via the Google ADK (Agent Development Kit). It acts as the cashier's "shift partner."
  • Supervisor Agent (Strategic Oversight): Powered by Gemini 2.5 Pro on Vertex AI Agent Engine. A2A (Agent-to-Agent) Protocol running over Cloud Pub/Sub.
  • And much more...

Visit www.somosvelora.com for more info

description_html:
Launching a product is not a single task, it’s a complex, multi-step process involving decision-making, planning, coordination, and execution across different roles like marketing, finance, and operations.

Today’s AI agents can assist with isolated tasks, writing content, generating plans, or answering questions but they fail when faced with real-world complexity:

  • They break in multi-step workflows
  • They cannot handle conflicting constraints (budget vs timeline vs quality)
  • They lack memory and coordination across roles
  • They stall or produce inconsistent outputs when conditions change

In reality, launching a product requires continuous adaptation, not static outputs.


👉 The core problem is not intelligence.

👉 The real problem is reliability in execution under dynamic conditions.

Our Solution


Rivtor: Autonomous Company Engine is a multi-agent system designed to simulate and execute real business workflows end-to-end from decision to planning to execution, while handling real-world uncertainty.


For this project, we focus on a single high-stakes workflow:


👉 Launching a product autonomously


Rivtor is composed of specialized agents (e.g., Marketing, Finance, Operations) that:

  • Interpret goals and make strategic decisions
  • Break down plans into structured, interdependent tasks
  • Execute actions (content creation, scheduling, task triggering, etc.)
  • Continuously monitor constraints like budget, time, and dependencies
  • Detect failures and conflicts during execution
  • Adapt and re-plan automatically to recover and continue

Unlike traditional agents, Rivtor does not stop at generating outputs.


It operates as a closed-loop system:


Decision → Planning → Execution → Failure Detection → Recovery → Continuation

What Makes Rivtor Different

  • End-to-End Execution
  • Goes beyond recommendations—Rivtor actually carries out workflows.
  • Multi-Agent Coordination
  • Simulates real organizational roles that collaborate and challenge each other.
  • Failure-Aware System
  • Designed to detect when plans break (budget overflow, timeline conflicts, missing inputs).
  • Self-Correcting Loop
  • Automatically adjusts decisions and plans without human intervention.
  • Observability & Transparency
  • Every decision, conflict, and correction is traceable, making the system debuggable and reliable.

Why It Matters

Most AI systems today are assistants.

Rivtor represents a shift toward:


👉 Autonomous execution systems that can operate in real-world environments


By focusing on reliability under complex, dynamic conditions, Rivtor addresses one of the biggest unsolved problems in AI agents: making them actually usable for real business operations.

Many students struggle to understand complex topics because most learning platforms and AI assistants provide only text-based explanations instead of visual and structured teaching. Learners often need diagrams, step-by-step guidance, real-world examples, and personalized explanations based on their pace and understanding level. Existing AI chatbots act as single agents and do not collaborate to create an interactive teaching experience. There is a need for a smarter learning assistant that explains concepts visually and adaptively like a real tutor.

Our solution

EduAgents is a multi-agent AI-powered learning assistant that helps students understand complex topics through conversation and visual explanations. Instead of using a single chatbot, EduAgents coordinates multiple specialized agents such as a Planner Agent, Teacher Agent, Visual Agent, Example Agent, and Quiz Agent to work together like a virtual teaching team. Students can ask questions naturally, and the system responds with structured explanations, diagrams, real-world examples, and quick assessments. This creates a personalized and interactive learning experience that makes difficult concepts easier to understand, especially for visual learners.

description_full: CausalMind Ops Engine- Detect, diagnose, patch, prove — self-healing pipelines for the modern data stack.Problem to solveCurrent tools stop at detection or, at best, root-cause localization - naming the failing service, table, or deploy. None close the loop: writing the patch, validating recovery against the business KPI, and signing an audit record. The senior data engineer still does that work at 3 AM.. Datadog detects a pipeline failed at 03:00 UTC, but cannot tell you that an upstream vendor renamed customer_status to status at 02:14 UTC and the fix is a four-line transformation patch. Monte Carlo flags the data anomaly but cannot write the SQL, regression test, or data contract. Sweep and Cursor agents wait for a human to type "fix this." Runbook automation runs deterministic playbooks for problems already understood. Every layer alerts or detects; none deploys a verified fix and turns the incident into a guardrail.This creates a massive ceiling on AI utility in B2B production reliability. Detecting that something broke is a parlor trick; closing the loop autonomously — with proven recovery and zero engineer minutes at 3 AM — is a highly monetizable enterprise service. The painful work of connecting an upstream vendor change to the downstream pipeline failure to the CFO's broken Monday dashboard, inferring the silently-shifted value mapping, generating the patch, validating recovery, and signing the SOX audit record still falls to the senior data engineer on call. Her reasoning stays locked inside her head, unscalable across thousands of pipelines.Mid-market data teams need a way to scale their gold-standard reliability reasoning without dilution. The market is large and underserved: AIOps is a $40B category and data contracts grow from $1.85B in 2024 to $8.72B by 2033 (Acceldata). Today's tools sit on the alert-and-detect side; the close-the-loop side is wide open.Our solutionCausalMind is a net-new autonomous reliability platform using the Agent Development Kit. Instead of stitching together monitoring, observability, code agents, and runbooks, we deploy a 12-agent specialist swarm that closes the loop end to end.A Coordinator — an ADK Custom Agent — orchestrates eleven specialists: Data, Log, Infra, API, Code Repair, Test, Validation, Learning, Governance, Deployment, and Cost. Each carries a narrow declarative system prompt and typed MCP toolset. Operating on declarative reliability ontologies — version-controlled data contracts, autonomy-tier policies, runbook RAG — they reason on customer-specific standards, not generic heuristics. Validation runs on a deliberately different model from Code Repair: a generator never grades its own work. The Coordinator runs the swarm in parallel for diagnosis, sequentially for remediation, and arbitrates outputs into an explicit causal graph. If Data flags a column rename in raw.customers at 02:14 UTC and Log flags a ColumnNotFound cluster at 02:31 UTC, the Coordinator diagnoses the vendor change as causal driver, generates the SQL patch, opens a GitHub PR under confidence-tiered autonomy, deploys after one human approval, validates the BigQuery dashboard recovers, and stores the signature so the next occurrence heals in seconds.Four capabilities differentiate CausalMind.Causal Lineage Diagnosis: reasoning over an explicit causal graph (APIs, BigQuery tables, Dataform actions, deployments, KPIs) instead of correlating log keywords.Drift Forecast: the Memory Agent predicts incidents by clustering vendor changelog cadence and signature similarity, surfacing risk days before impact.Time-Travel Debug: every step writes an immutable event to an append-only timeline — replayable as compliance evidence, debug surface, and audit record in one artifact.Confidence-Tiered Autonomy: trust climbs from recommend-only, through PR with human merge, to auto-merge of low-risk patterns with 50+ prior successes.By separating agent reasoning (Gemini + ADK + MCP) from operational knowledge (contracts, autonomy policies, runbook RAG, timelines), data teams, CISOs, and CFOs audit, edit, and scale their exact reliability standards without dilution. Every fix carries a cryptographic provenance record signed at deploy — transforming subjective 3 AM triage into SOX-grade documentation. Reference customer Helio Finance, a mid-market fintech, spends ~$416K/year on drift triage; CausalMind targets 80% elimination at $48K/year — a 7× year-one return.Powered by Gemini Enterprise Agent Platform end to end. Deployed to Vertex AI Agent Runtime; Sessions hold per-incident state, Memory Bank manages long-term pattern similarity. Reasoning runs on Gemini 2.5 Pro and 2.0 Flash. Custom RAG grounds against Agent Search Data Stores (BigQuery-backed) for runbooks and contracts; Grounding with Google Search powers Drift Forecast by monitoring vendor changelogs. Discoverable via A2A at /.well-known/agent.json so other agents in a Gemini Enterprise mesh can query CausalMind in natural language.

Datadog detects a pipeline failed at 03:00 UTC, but cannot tell you that an upstream vendor renamed customer_status to status at 02:14 UTC and the fix is a four-line transformation patch. Mid-market data teams need a way to scale their gold-standard reliability reasoning without dilution. Today's tools sit on the alert-and-detect side; the close-the-loop side is wide open.

Our solution

CausalMind is a net-new autonomous reliability platform using the Agent Development Kit. Instead of stitching together monitoring, observability, code agents, and runbooks, we deploy a 12-agent specialist swarm that closes the loop end to end.


A Coordinator — an ADK Custom Agent — orchestrates eleven specialists: Data, Log, Infra, API, Code Repair, Test, Validation, Learning, Governance, Deployment, and Cost. Each carries a narrow declarative system prompt and typed MCP toolset. Operating on declarative reliability ontologies — version-controlled data contracts, autonomy-tier policies, runbook RAG — they reason on customer-specific standards, not generic heuristics. The Coordinator runs the swarm in parallel for diagnosis, sequentially for remediation, and arbitrates outputs into an explicit causal graph. If Data flags a column rename in raw.customers at 02:14 UTC and Log flags a ColumnNotFound cluster at 02:31 UTC, the Coordinator diagnoses the vendor change as causal driver, generates the SQL patch, opens a GitHub PR under confidence-tiered autonomy, deploys after one human approval, validates the BigQuery dashboard recovers, and stores the signature so the next occurrence heals in seconds.


Four capabilities differentiate CausalMind.

Causal Lineage Diagnosis: reasoning over an explicit causal graph (APIs, BigQuery tables, Dataform actions, deployments, KPIs) instead of correlating log keywords.

Drift Forecast: the Memory Agent predicts incidents by clustering vendor changelog cadence and signature similarity, surfacing risk days before impact.

Time-Travel Debug: every step writes an immutable event to an append-only timeline — replayable as compliance evidence, debug surface, and audit record in one artifact.

Confidence-Tiered Autonomy: trust climbs from recommend-only, through PR with human merge, to auto-merge of low-risk patterns with 50+ prior successes.


By separating agent reasoning (Gemini + ADK + MCP) from operational knowledge (contracts, autonomy policies, runbook RAG, timelines), data teams, CISOs, and CFOs audit, edit, and scale their exact reliability standards without dilution. Every fix carries a cryptographic provenance record signed at deploy — transforming subjective 3 AM triage into SOX-grade documentation. Reference customer Helio Finance, a mid-market fintech, spends ~$416K/year on drift triage; CausalMind targets 80% elimination at $48K/year — a 7× year-one return.


Powered by Gemini Enterprise Agent Platform end to end. Deployed to Vertex AI Agent Runtime; Sessions hold per-incident state, Memory Bank manages long-term pattern similarity. Reasoning runs on Gemini 2.5 Pro and 2.0 Flash. Custom RAG grounds against Agent Search Data Stores (BigQuery-backed) for runbooks and contracts; Grounding with Google Search powers Drift Forecast by monitoring vendor changelogs. Discoverable via A2A at /.well-known/agent.json so other agents in a Gemini Enterprise mesh can query CausalMind in natural language.

description_html:
Problem to solve


Behind-the-wheel lessons should be learning experiences, not pass/fail moments or overwhelming correction lists.


In a good drive lesson, the student is practicing skills and drills, learning as they go, and receiving simple follow-up from the instructor: “work on this next.” But in real-world instruction, two problems can happen. Some lessons drift toward evaluation-heavy instruction, where the student feels like every drive is a test. Other lessons end with too many corrections at once, leaving the student discouraged or unsure what actually matters most.


Students need clear, respectful feedback they can digest. A young adult, international student, or student without strong practice support at home may need more than “brake earlier” or “watch your speed.” They need to understand the specific skill to work on, why it matters, and what practice step comes next.


OpenDrive Clipboard helps licensed instructors turn teachable moments into instructor-reviewed debriefs that are simple, structured, and evidence-informed. A moment such as taking right turns too fast, missing signals, drifting into one-handed control, following too closely, or braking late can become a clear practice focus without turning the debrief into criticism or shame.


The goal is not to replace instructor judgment. The goal is to help instructors preserve the lesson, connect observations to specific skills, and send students home with respectful next steps they can actually use.


AI organizes the moment. The instructor leads the lesson.


Our solution


OpenDrive Clipboard is a Gemini-powered, ADK-ready agent workflow that drafts post-drive debrief materials for a licensed instructor to review and approve.


It runs after the lesson, not during the drive. It does not exist in the vehicle cabin. It does not capture interior audio. It does not control the vehicle, grade readiness, make licensing decisions, or communicate with the student during a drive.


The agent takes a demo driving scenario, such as late braking near a crosswalk, residential speed creep, following too closely, missed signaling, or yellow-light indecision, and produces an instructor-ready draft containing:


1. Safety summary

2. Observed safety concern

3. Suggested lesson focus

4. Student reflection prompt

5. Practice assignment

6. Family-friendly summary

7. Language-access preview

8. Reviewed demo log entry


Every output is marked: DRAFT — INSTRUCTOR REVIEW REQUIRED.


The draft is held at a hard human-review gate. The licensed instructor clicks Approve, Edit, Reject, or Regenerate. Nothing reaches a student or family without the instructor’s sign-off. This is the product’s safety boundary, regulatory boundary, and core differentiator.


Architecture:


- Gemini for reasoning and classification

- Google ADK / ADK-ready orchestration

- Typed tool layer for demo drive events, lesson-context retrieval, debrief drafting, language-access preview, and instructor review decisions

- Vue 3 + Laravel instructor review-gate dashboard

- Google Cloud Run deployment for the dashboard and agent services


The public demo uses demo data only: no real student records, no PII, no production OpenDrive Beacon data, no interior audio, and no production secrets in the public repo.


Two products, one umbrella:


OpenDrive Beacon is the private in-vehicle evidence layer: CAN bus, IMU, GPS, and forward-road camera data for future instructor-reviewed post-drive analysis.


OpenDrive Clipboard is this public hackathon submission: the post-drive drafting and review layer that turns demo driving scenarios into instructor-reviewed debrief materials.


Beacon records. Clipboard drafts. The licensed instructor decides.


Regulatory posture:


OpenDrive Clipboard supports licensed-instructor documentation and review. AI-assisted note drafting is treated as a documentation aid for the licensed professional, similar to a digital intake form, structured event log, or lesson-summary drafting aid.


The system is not an AI driving instructor, vehicle-control system, licensing evaluator, diagnostic tool, enforcement tool, or readiness grader.


OpenDrive™, OpenDrive Beacon™, and OpenDrive Clipboard™ are working names of Street Law OpenDrive.

description_full: Problem to solveProblem to solveSpanish accounting firms do not just struggle with reading invoices; they struggle with operating high-volume, multi-client invoice workflows across email, WhatsApp, drives, portals, and accounting systems. Manual batch review, tax coding, exception handling, missing-document follow-up, and tenant-by-tenant supervision make the process slow, expensive, difficult to scale, and prone to errors.The urgency is growing because Spain is moving toward mandatory B2B e-invoicing under Ley Crea y Crece, while VAT record books already have formal AEAT requirements and some taxpayers must manage VAT reporting through SII. As a result, firms need more than OCR: they need an operational layer that can review batches of invoices, monitor tenant health, detect anomalies, and keep document workflows under control with full traceability.Our solutionWe are building a production-grade AI backoffice agent for Spanish accounting firms on Google Cloud. Instead of using the LLM to read every invoice expensively, the agent uses our proprietary invoice-processing pipeline as a specialized low-cost perception layer, and combines it with Gemini 2.5 and the Agent Development Kit to orchestrate document intake, batch invoice review, tenant supervision, exception handling, and repetitive tax backoffice operations at scale.The agent is designed to supervise thousands of invoices across multiple tenants, not just process one document at a time. It can collect invoices from multiple channels, trigger bulk processing jobs, review low-confidence or inconsistent outputs, monitor invoice and tenant status across periods, flag anomalies, request missing documents, escalate edge cases to a human reviewer, and push validated results into the accounting workflow.

description_full: An optimization agent that stress-tests developer-role matching, detects weak fit scores, and improves shortlist reliability using AI interview evidence.Problem to solveTechnology companies do not just need faster developer matching. They need matching they can trust.A candidate can look strong on a resume but fail in real technical reasoning. Another candidate can look average on paper but be a strong fit after reviewing GitHub, project history, salary expectations, timezone, and AI technical interview evidence.This creates a serious reliability problem for hiring agents. If the matching agent over-trusts resumes, ignores missing evidence, or fails on edge cases, the company receives weak-fit candidates with confident explanations. That creates false positives, wastes CTO and senior engineer time, and turns hiring back into a lottery.This is especially dangerous in technical hiring because the cost of a wrong engineering hire is high: months of lost development, salary, onboarding, team distraction, and another hiring cycle.The challenge is not only to match candidates faster. The challenge is to make the matching agent reliable under real-world edge cases.Our solutionWe are building WorkorAI Matching Reliability Agent — an optimization agent that improves the reliability of developer-role matching.The agent stress-tests WorkorAI matching logic against difficult hiring scenarios: incomplete resumes, inflated skill claims, missing salary data, timezone conflicts, mismatched seniority, strong GitHub but weak interview performance, or strong interview evidence but weak resume structure.It compares candidate claims from CV, GitHub, and LinkedIn against AI technical interview evidence and the role scorecard. Then it detects weak reasoning, flags unsupported fit scores, identifies missing evidence, and improves the final match report.The output is not just a ranked shortlist. It is a reliability-checked shortlist.Retrieval layer: RAG with gemini-embedding-003Each job and candidate profile is ingested as semantically chunked documents with explicit metadata boundaries for stack, seniority, locale, and AI interview transcript. Embeddings are generated using `gemini-embedding-003` and stored in a vector index. For each job↔candidate pair, vector search retrieves the most relevant evidence context and feeds it directly into the reasoning layer.Reasoning layer: Gemini 3 ProRetrieved evidence feeds a Gemini 3 Pro reasoning layer on top of WorkorAI's own matching model. It evaluates the full evidence picture — technical depth from GitHub, behavioral signals from the AI interview, stated expectations vs. role requirements — and produces a corrected, explainable match report.Each candidate report includes:— original fit score — corrected fit score — confidence level — missing evidence — risk notes — reasoning issues detected — AI interview evidence — recommended follow-up questionsDemo scenario:Before optimization, a candidate receives an 88% fit score because the resume lists Python, FastAPI, PostgreSQL, and AWS.After the Matching Reliability Agent runs, the score is corrected to 71% because the AI technical interview shows weak system design reasoning, limited production AWS experience, and unclear ownership of backend architecture.The agent then explains the risks and generates follow-up interview questions for the CTO.ResultWorkorAI does not only match developers faster. It makes matching safer, more explainable, and more reliable for real hiring decisions.
description_html:
An optimization agent that stress-tests developer-role matching, detects weak fit scores, and improves shortlist reliability using AI interview evidence.


Problem to solve


Technology companies do not just need faster developer matching. They need matching they can trust.


A candidate can look strong on a resume but fail in real technical reasoning. Another candidate can look average on paper but be a strong fit after reviewing GitHub, project history, salary expectations, timezone, and AI technical interview evidence.


This creates a serious reliability problem for hiring agents. If the matching agent over-trusts resumes, ignores missing evidence, or fails on edge cases, the company receives weak-fit candidates with confident explanations. That creates false positives, wastes CTO and senior engineer time, and turns hiring back into a lottery.


This is especially dangerous in technical hiring because the cost of a wrong engineering hire is high: months of lost development, salary, onboarding, team distraction, and another hiring cycle.


The challenge is not only to match candidates faster. The challenge is to make the matching agent reliable under real-world edge cases.


Our solution


We are building WorkorAI Matching Reliability Agent — an optimization agent that improves the reliability of developer-role matching.


The agent stress-tests WorkorAI matching logic against difficult hiring scenarios: incomplete resumes, inflated skill claims, missing salary data, timezone conflicts, mismatched seniority, strong GitHub but weak interview performance, or strong interview evidence but weak resume structure.


It compares candidate claims from CV, GitHub, and LinkedIn against AI technical interview evidence and the role scorecard. Then it detects weak reasoning, flags unsupported fit scores, identifies missing evidence, and improves the final match report.


The output is not just a ranked shortlist. It is a reliability-checked shortlist.


Retrieval layer: RAG with gemini-embedding-003


Each job and candidate profile is ingested as semantically chunked documents with explicit metadata boundaries for stack, seniority, locale, and AI interview transcript. Embeddings are generated using `gemini-embedding-003` and stored in a vector index. For each job↔candidate pair, vector search retrieves the most relevant evidence context and feeds it directly into the reasoning layer.


Reasoning layer: Gemini 3 Pro


Retrieved evidence feeds a Gemini 3 Pro reasoning layer on top of WorkorAI's own matching model. It evaluates the full evidence picture — technical depth from GitHub, behavioral signals from the AI interview, stated expectations vs. role requirements — and produces a corrected, explainable match report.


Each candidate report includes:


— original fit score

— corrected fit score

— confidence level

— missing evidence

— risk notes

— reasoning issues detected

— AI interview evidence

— recommended follow-up questions


Demo scenario:


Before optimization, a candidate receives an 88% fit score because the resume lists Python, FastAPI, PostgreSQL, and AWS.


After the Matching Reliability Agent runs, the score is corrected to 71% because the AI technical interview shows weak system design reasoning, limited production AWS experience, and unclear ownership of backend architecture.


The agent then explains the risks and generates follow-up interview questions for the CTO.


Result


WorkorAI does not only match developers faster. It makes matching safer, more explainable, and more reliable for real hiring decisions.

description_html:
Ready to start building? Check out this guide for next steps.

Problem to solve


Modern farming faces a perfect storm of pressures: unpredictable climate, depleted water tables, rising input costs, labor shortages, and volatile markets. Existing tools tackle small slices of this problem but rarely connect them.


Pain Points

  • Generic regional weather forecasts miss what is actually happening on a single field.
  • Over-irrigation wastes up to 50% of agricultural water in many regions; under-irrigation kills yield.
  • Disease and pest outbreaks are usually detected only after visible damage, when it is too late.
  • Smallholder farmers cannot afford enterprise platforms like Climate FieldView or Taranis.
  • Most digital tools are dashboards. They show data but do not make decisions or take action.
  • Language and literacy barriers leave huge populations of farmers unable to use existing apps.

Our solution


AgriMind is a cloud-hosted AI agent that pairs a reasoning engine with a library of specialized agronomy modules and a real-time alert system. It pulls live data from public weather APIs, optional on-farm sensors, and a built-in crop-knowledge database, then continuously asks itself: what is the best action for this field, this crop, this hour?

The agent communicates with farmers through a lightweight mobile-first web app, SMS, WhatsApp, voice calls, or smart-speaker integration. When the agent has authority and connected hardware, it executes actions directly — opening an irrigation valve, dispatching a drone, or placing an input order. When it does not, it sends a clear alert with one-tap approval.

description_html:
An MCP-powered hiring agent that lets company AI agents create hiring requests and receive verified developer matches for specific engineering roles.


Problem to solve


Technology companies worldwide receive hundreds or even thousands of applications for engineering roles, but the best candidates often get lost among weak-fit profiles.


For CTOs, founders, VP Engineering, and recruiting teams, hiring developers becomes like searching for a needle in a haystack. Teams manually review resumes, check tech stacks, assess experience, salary expectations, time zones, and technical depth.


The cost of a wrong hire is high. When there is not enough verified data about a candidate, hiring becomes a lottery: decisions are made based on a resume, a short call, and a subjective impression. A bad engineering hire can cost months of lost development, salary, onboarding, team time, and another hiring cycle.


Companies have already tried job boards, LinkedIn, ATS tools, recruiters, and AI agents. But these tools either increase the flow of profiles, store applications, or require manual communication. Company AI agents still do not have a secure way to create a hiring request and receive verified, explainable matching for a specific role.


Our solution


We are building WorkorAI Employer Hiring Agent — an MCP-powered agent interface for company AI agents.


WorkorAI is a developer hiring platform that verifies candidates through CV, GitHub, LinkedIn, and AI technical interviews, then matches them with engineering roles through explainable matching.


An employer creates a workspace in WorkorAI, receives an API key, and connects it to their AI agent. After that, the agent can describe a role in natural language, send a hiring request through MCP, and receive a shortlist of developers who actually fit that role within minutes.


Each match includes:


— fit score

— tech stack fit

— salary fit

— timezone fit

— key match reasons

— risks

— evidence from the AI technical interview

— questions for the next interview


Demo scenario


A CTO writes to their AI agent:


“Find me a senior backend engineer for a remote AI product team. We need Python, FastAPI, PostgreSQL, AWS, product engineering experience, and availability in GMT+2–GMT+7.”


Through WorkorAI Employer Hiring Agent, the agent creates a hiring request, sends the role description to WorkorAI, receives structured hiring intent, requests matched developers, and returns an explainable shortlist.


Result: the company no longer searches for a needle in a haystack manually. Its AI agent gets verified matching for a specific engineering role within minutes.

description_html:
A self-hosted AI hiring system that runs inside the customer’s own Google Cloud environment, so candidate data, interview transcripts, and fit scores stay under the company’s control.


Problem to solve


Enterprise technical hiring has a data security problem.


AI hiring tools can now screen, interview, and match developers at scale. But large companies cannot easily use third-party hiring SaaS when the workflow requires sending candidate CVs, AI interview transcripts, internal salary bands, role requirements, and fit scores into an external vendor environment.


For legal, security, and compliance teams, this creates a hard blocker. Hiring data is sensitive: it includes candidate identity, technical performance, compensation expectations, workforce planning, and internal role requirements.


The result is a bad tradeoff: enterprises either keep slow manual hiring workflows or use AI hiring tools while accepting sensitive data exposure.


There is no simple enterprise-ready way to run an agent-native AI hiring system inside the company’s own infrastructure, on the company’s own keys, without routing sensitive hiring data through a third-party platform.


Our solution


We are building WorkorAI Enterprise Self-Hosted Hiring Agent — an AI hiring system that runs inside the customer’s own Google Cloud environment.


The system verifies developers through CV, GitHub, LinkedIn, and AI technical interviews, then matches them with engineering roles through explainable fit scoring. In the enterprise version, the entire workflow runs inside the customer’s perimeter.


Instead of sending candidates to an external platform, the company invites them into its own private hiring instance. The AI interview, profile analysis, semantic matching, reliability scoring, match report, evidence, risks, and follow-up questions are generated locally.


The system is designed for Google Cloud Marketplace and Gemini Enterprise:


— deployed on Google Cloud via GKE or Cloud Run

— Gemini 3 Pro runs on the customer’s own Google Cloud project and API keys

— semantic matching uses gemini-embedding-003

— A2A protocol enables discovery and coordination with enterprise agents

— candidate data, interview transcripts, and fit scores stay inside the customer’s environment

— audit trail supports legal, security, and compliance review


Demo scenario


A company deploys the system inside its own Google Cloud project.


Its internal HR agent sends a hiring request for a senior backend engineer. Candidates receive a direct link to the company’s private AI interviewer. The interview runs inside the company’s infrastructure. The matching agent processes CV, GitHub, LinkedIn, and interview evidence locally.


The CTO receives a verified shortlist with fit scores, evidence, risks, and follow-up questions — without candidate data ever leaving the company’s perimeter.


Result


Enterprises get agent-native technical hiring without the data governance risk.


Their cloud. Their keys. Their candidate data. Their hiring agents.

description_full: Ku-AI-Sales-System is a backend AI agent system that helps B2B sales teams find potential customers and write personalised cold outreach to them - automatically.A sales team starts by describing its product. From there, the system does three things:Finds leads — it searches directories like Crunchbase, G2, and LinkedIn to find companies that match the ideal customer profile.Researches each lead — it crawls the target company's website, social media, and news to build a detailed profile: who works there, who makes buying decisions, what tools they use, and what problems they might have.Writes the outreach — using everything it learned, it drafts two A/B-tested cold emails and a LinkedIn message for each decision maker, personalised to their role, interests, and pain points.
description_html:
Ku-AI-Sales-System is a backend AI agent system that helps B2B sales teams find potential customers and write personalised cold outreach to them - automatically.


A sales team starts by describing its product. From there, the system does three things:

  1. Finds leads — it searches directories like Crunchbase, G2, and LinkedIn to find companies that match the ideal customer profile.
  2. Researches each lead — it crawls the target company's website, social media, and news to build a detailed profile: who works there, who makes buying decisions, what tools they use, and what problems they might have.
  3. Writes the outreach — using everything it learned, it drafts two A/B-tested cold emails and a LinkedIn message for each decision maker, personalised to their role, interests, and pain points.

project_name: Synapse: Shared memory for your AI agent fleet
team_names: Joseph Thomas; Tanishq Kaul; Marisha Boyd
description_full: Your AI agents have amnesia. Join the team curing it!Problem to solveAI agents are powerful but stateless. Every conversation starts from zero. They don't know what you watched last week, what your team decided in Tuesday's meeting, or which of your anchor concepts a new idea connects to. Meanwhile, knowledge workers ingest huge volumes of unstructured content (meetings, YouTube, docs, notes) that sit in silos and never get cross-referenced. The result: agents can't act as real thinking partners because they have no access to the context that makes you you. Giving every agent its own memory store doesn't scale either. What's missing is a shared, structured, queryable knowledge substrate that any agent can read from.Our solutionSynapse is a personal knowledge graph that functions as the shared memory layer for AI agent fleets. It ingests content from meetings, YouTube playlists, documents, and notes, then uses Gemini 2.5 Flash to extract typed entities and relationships across 24 categories, and Gemini text-embedding-004 to make every passage semantically retrievable. The graph is exposed to any MCP-compatible agent (Claude Desktop, Claude Code, custom runtimes) via 15+ tools including ask_synapse, search_entities, get_connections, and get_source_content.The loop compounds: every source you add makes every agent smarter, every query richer, and every cross-connection more likely to surface the non-obvious link that matters.
description_html:
Your AI agents have amnesia. Join the team curing it!

Problem to solve

AI agents are powerful but stateless. Every conversation starts from zero. They don't know what you watched last week, what your team decided in Tuesday's meeting, or which of your anchor concepts a new idea connects to. Meanwhile, knowledge workers ingest huge volumes of unstructured content (meetings, YouTube, docs, notes) that sit in silos and never get cross-referenced. The result: agents can't act as real thinking partners because they have no access to the context that makes you you. Giving every agent its own memory store doesn't scale either. What's missing is a shared, structured, queryable knowledge substrate that any agent can read from.

Our solution

Synapse is a personal knowledge graph that functions as the shared memory layer for AI agent fleets. It ingests content from meetings, YouTube playlists, documents, and notes, then uses Gemini 2.5 Flash to extract typed entities and relationships across 24 categories, and Gemini text-embedding-004 to make every passage semantically retrievable. The graph is exposed to any MCP-compatible agent (Claude Desktop, Claude Code, custom runtimes) via 15+ tools including ask_synapse, search_entities, get_connections, and get_source_content.

The loop compounds: every source you add makes every agent smarter, every query richer, and every cross-connection more likely to surface the non-obvious link that matters.

description_full: Problem to solve Standard AI chatbots degrade the educational process by instantly handing out answers instead of guiding a student's reasoning. Furthermore, learning is inherently visual and spatial, but current AI interactions are trapped in linear, text-based chat interfaces. Students don't just need an answer key; they need a dedicated mentor who can visually "see" their work, draw diagrams to explain complex concepts, and guide them through a structured, Socratic learning process without breaking conversational flow.Our solution We are building Tablo, a real-time, multimodal Socratic AI whiteboard. Tablo replaces the traditional chatbox with a shared digital canvas. Using a sub-second WebRTC voice loop paired with Gemini Live, the AI communicates via natural voice. Through a triggered board-snapshot system, the agent maintains spatial awareness of the student's work without exhausting context windows. The agent can natively execute deterministic commands to draw 20+ types of diagrams (SVGs, 3D shapes, math graphs) directly on the board alongside the student. Bounded by strict Socratic prompting, the agent acts as a true tutor. We will leverage Google Cloud infrastructure to transition Tablo to production-grade reliability, utilizing Gemini for core reasoning and multimodal RAG to securely ground the agent's knowledge in user-uploaded textbooks.

Students struggle to connect theoretical knowledge with clinical reasoning, lack personalized case-based practice, and spend excessive time searching multiple sources instead of learning in a structured way. It acts like a virtual clinical tutor:

The system is built using Gemini-powered agents and designed to operate as a multi-step autonomous learning assistant rather than a static Q&A tool.

description_full: MentorIA Municipal Agents is a multi-agent orchestration system that turns the citizen-government interface into a single conversational entry point. Instead of forcing citizens to navigate fragmented portals, paper forms, and disconnected departments, our agents collaborate to interpret requests, validate documents, ground answers in municipal law, and route cases to the right department — all through WhatsApp or web chat in natural Spanish.Built on Google's Agent Development Kit (ADK) with Gemini 3.1 as the reasoning engine, the system orchestrates three specialized agents communicating via the A2A protocol: a Normative Agent grounded in municipal ordinances and Dominican constitutional law via Vertex AI Search, a Document Validator leveraging Gemini's native multimodal capabilities to verify IDs and permits, and a Routing Agent that connects to the municipality's case management system through a custom MCP server. The system is deployed on Cloud Run with Cloud Trace for observability, and produces an immutable audit trail for every decision.The pilot deployment targets the Ayuntamiento de La Romana under a signed Declaration of Interest with the municipal government. The architecture is designed to be replicated across Latin American municipalities through a "Digital Government Franchise" model promoted by IDECON 3x7, a Dominican civil society organization participating in the Google.org AI for Government Innovation Accelerator 2026 cohort.

description_full: TrialMatch is a multi-agent AI system that allows a single clinical research coordinator to deeply steward 100+ complex patient profiles across thousands of global trials—instead of 5–10—by autonomously running the recruitment chain (Profile → Search → Logic → Action). We use Agentic Workflows, Long-Context Reasoning, and the Model Context Protocol (MCP) to automate the high-stakes eligibility matching that currently causes 85% of clinical trials to fail their enrollment targets.The Business Use CaseClinical research is a $70B+ industry where the primary failure point is not science, but administrative latency. We analyzed the recruitment workflow at major oncology centers and identified a universal pattern:The Cognitive Saturation Point: Research nurses can only deeply steward a handful of patients at a time because the cognitive load of cross-referencing 100-page trial protocols against messy patient charts is a manual "search and compare" task.The "Analyst" Gap: Existing tools are static databases (search engines). They require the human to do the reading. There is no "Intelligence Layer" that reads the protocol for the nurse and justifies the match.The Cost of Silence: A single day of recruitment delay costs pharmaceutical sponsors between $600k and $8M in potential daily revenue. For the patient, it is the difference between a life-saving intervention and running out of time.
description_html:
TrialMatch is a multi-agent AI system that allows a single clinical research coordinator to deeply steward 100+ complex patient profiles across thousands of global trials—instead of 5–10—by autonomously running the recruitment chain (Profile → Search → Logic → Action). We use Agentic Workflows, Long-Context Reasoning, and the Model Context Protocol (MCP) to automate the high-stakes eligibility matching that currently causes 85% of clinical trials to fail their enrollment targets.


The Business Use Case

Clinical research is a $70B+ industry where the primary failure point is not science, but administrative latency. We analyzed the recruitment workflow at major oncology centers and identified a universal pattern:

  • The Cognitive Saturation Point: Research nurses can only deeply steward a handful of patients at a time because the cognitive load of cross-referencing 100-page trial protocols against messy patient charts is a manual "search and compare" task.
  • The "Analyst" Gap: Existing tools are static databases (search engines). They require the human to do the reading. There is no "Intelligence Layer" that reads the protocol for the nurse and justifies the match.
  • The Cost of Silence: A single day of recruitment delay costs pharmaceutical sponsors between $600k and $8M in potential daily revenue. For the patient, it is the difference between a life-saving intervention and running out of time.

project_name: KidTok - Lessons on Any Topic That Your Kid Loves to Finish
description_full: Problem to solveParents desperately want their children's screen time to matter, but the only alternative to low-quality YouTube is generic EdTech that doesn't hold a 4-year-old's attention for 30 seconds. There's no tool that lets a parent say "I want my child to learn curiosity about science, the way I am" and get back something their child will actually finish watching. The content that kids love (short-form video) and the content parents want them to consume (educational, values-aligned) have never overlapped - until now.My solutionKidTok is a multi-agent AI system that transforms any parent's intent into a fully produced, ~30-60-second personalized video lesson for their child in under 5 minutes. A parent describes a topic, their child's age, and what values they want to reinforce. An orchestrated pipeline of specialized agents, built on Google ADK and powered by Gemini, takes it from there:• A Curriculum Planner Agent scaffolds age-appropriate content using co-designed pedagogy (hook → learn → play → micro-reward), shaped by a 40-year kindergarten teacher.• A Content Agent generates narration lines, quiz moments, and values tagging via Gemini.• A Narration Agent calls ElevenLabs TTS via MCP tool connection.• A Visual Asset Agent generates scene-by-scene imagery.• A Video Composer Agent assembles the final personalized lesson.The result is not a YouTube recommendation, but a lesson built specifically for that child, by that parent's intent. KidTok is live in Beta and has paying customers since May 2, 2026.
description_html:
Problem to solve

Parents desperately want their children's screen time to matter, but the only alternative to low-quality YouTube is generic EdTech that doesn't hold a 4-year-old's attention for 30 seconds. There's no tool that lets a parent say "I want my child to learn curiosity about science, the way I am" and get back something their child will actually finish watching. The content that kids love (short-form video) and the content parents want them to consume (educational, values-aligned) have never overlapped - until now.

My solution

KidTok is a multi-agent AI system that transforms any parent's intent into a fully produced, ~30-60-second personalized video lesson for their child in under 5 minutes. A parent describes a topic, their child's age, and what values they want to reinforce. An orchestrated pipeline of specialized agents, built on Google ADK and powered by Gemini, takes it from there:


• A Curriculum Planner Agent scaffolds age-appropriate content using co-designed pedagogy (hook → learn → play → micro-reward), shaped by a 40-year kindergarten teacher.

• A Content Agent generates narration lines, quiz moments, and values tagging via Gemini.

• A Narration Agent calls ElevenLabs TTS via MCP tool connection.

• A Visual Asset Agent generates scene-by-scene imagery.

• A Video Composer Agent assembles the final personalized lesson.


The result is not a YouTube recommendation, but a lesson built specifically for that child, by that parent's intent. KidTok is live in Beta and has paying customers since May 2, 2026.

description_html:
Problem to solve


Legal drafting is an extremely time and effort-consuming process. Despite 80% digital coverage, over 20% of legal precedents go unused due to the high friction of procedural validation. Lawyers spend days drafting, only for documents to be rejected due to minor formatting or jurisdictional errors. Legal drafting is a structurally constrained professional work, yet most AI systems treat it like generic text generation. Legal documents must adhere to rigid procedural rules, formatting standards, and jurisdiction-specific constraints; even minor errors result in rejected filings, sanctions, or loss of trust. Legal documents must adhere to rigid procedural rules, formatting standards, and jurisdiction-specific constraints; even minor errors result in rejected filings, sanctions, or loss of trust. Current AI tools treat legal work as "generic text," leading to:

  • High Rejection Rates: Up to 12% of filings fail due to clerical and procedural defects.
  • Operational Burnout: The loop of rejection -> manual correction -> resubmission is a massive drain on resources.
  • Hallucination Risks: Unverified AI drafting destroys trust and risks legal sanctions
  • Procedural Compliance: Automating court-specific filing requirements.
  • Deterministic Formatting: Ensuring "clerk-ready" document structure.
  • Grounding: Preventing fabricated citations and ensuring auditability.


Key Fact: Despite 80% digital coverage of legal precedents, only 20% are effectively utilized because the cost of manually validating AI-generated outputs against these datasets is prohibitive. Most existing systems are merely "prompt-response" wrappers that lack adversarial validation.


Our solution



Clausely is a Multi-Agent Legal Intelligence System built on the Google ADK to automate the path from draft to guaranteed acceptance. We prioritize Google Enterprise integration, delivering a native Google Docs Add-on, Chrome Extension, and MS Word Add-in:

  • Gemini (Lead Agent): Provides high-fidelity adversarial validation and tool-calling to ensure court-ready precision.
  • Gemma (Edge Agent): Drives our cost-effective, sovereign drafting layer for offline and privacy-first tiers.
  • Google Cloud Marketplace: Architected as a Net-New Engine using ADK, designed for enterprise distribution and featured partner status.

By treating Law as Code, Clausely shortens the cycle of rejection and improvement, making high-stakes drafting 70% faster and resource-sustainable.

description_html:
DIFARYX is an autonomous scientific agent that transforms fragmented experimental data into structured, decision-ready insight.


Instead of relying on manual interpretation across disconnected tools, the system executes analysis, aligns evidence, and produces conclusions as part of a unified workflow. This introduces a new autonomous agent layer built specifically during the challenge, enabling goal-driven reasoning, tool orchestration, and explainable decision-making.

Problem to solve

Modern materials discovery is no longer limited by data generation.

It is limited by interpretation.


Across research labs and industrial R&D, large volumes of experimental data are produced every day. However, transforming this data into reliable scientific conclusions remains a fundamentally manual process.

Today’s workflow is fragmented by design.


Each technique operates in isolation. XRD, XPS, FTIR, and Raman are analyzed in separate tools, with different assumptions and interfaces. Researchers are forced to manually compare outputs, resolve inconsistencies, and synthesize conclusions based on experience.


This creates systemic limitations.

  • Interpretation is slow and inconsistent. The same dataset can lead to different conclusions depending on the analyst.
  • Knowledge remains siloed across tools, preventing integrated understanding.
  • There is no unified layer that connects evidence into a coherent interpretation.
  • Critical insights are often missed or discovered too late.
  • Reproducibility and traceability are weak.

Despite advances in AI, there is still no system capable of reasoning across multi-modal scientific data to produce structured, explainable decisions.

This limitation is not incremental. It is structural. Scientific workflows have scaled in data generation, but not in understanding. As datasets grow in complexity and dimensionality, manual interpretation becomes a bottleneck that slows discovery, increases cost, and reduces confidence in results. Solving this problem requires more than better tools. It requires a system that can reason across data.

Our solution

DIFARYX is an autonomous scientific agent that executes interpretation as part of the workflow, not after it. While DIFARYX provides a scientific analysis environment, this introduces a new agent layer that transforms the system from a tool into a reasoning engine.


The system operates from declarative intent. Users define what they want to understand, not how to perform the analysis. The agent determines the workflow, selects the appropriate tools, executes operations, and produces a final decision. This shifts the paradigm from predefined pipelines to dynamic, goal-driven execution.


How It Works

The agent operates through a dynamic execution model:

Input → Context → Execution → Fusion → Reasoning → Decision

  • Data is ingested from multiple experimental sources and organized into a shared context.
  • The agent constructs a workflow at runtime, selecting and executing analysis tools through MCP-style connections.
  • Outputs from different techniques are aligned into a unified evidence space.
  • The reasoning layer evaluates consistency, compares hypotheses, and resolves conflicts.
  • A final decision is produced with confidence and supporting evidence.

This process is iterative and adaptive. The agent continues execution until the interpretation reaches internal consistency.


Key Capabilities

• Declarative intent execution, where objectives define the workflow

• Dynamic orchestration of tools and data sources

• Multi-modal alignment of experimental signals

• Autonomous reasoning across combined evidence

• Structured outputs with confidence and explanation

• Full traceability of every decision


Technology Stack

The system is built in alignment with the challenge requirements.

• Gemini via Vertex AI for reasoning and hypothesis evaluation

• Custom agent orchestration layer inspired by ADK principles

• MCP-style tool interface for connecting analysis modules

• Deployment on Google Cloud (Cloud Run)

• DIFARYX scientific workspace as the frontend interface


Impact

DIFARYX removes the need for manual interpretation across disconnected tools. The system maintains context across all steps, allowing outputs from one analysis to directly inform the next. New data can be incorporated dynamically, enabling interpretation to evolve as evidence grows. Because execution is driven by intent rather than predefined logic, the same system adapts across datasets without reconfiguration. This enables faster interpretation, consistent results, improved reproducibility, and scalable scientific decision-making.
