description_html:
Ready to start building? Check out this guide for next steps.

Problem to solve

Pharmaceutical companies lose $1–8 million per day when FDA drug approvals are delayed — and 30–40% of those delays are caused not by bad science, but by non-compliant clinical trial data.

Clinical data teams run hundreds of pipelines daily across Airflow, dbt, and Snowflake. When these pipelines fail or produce data that violates FDA CDISC SDTM/ADaM standards, failures go undetected for 4–6 hours on average — discovered only when a downstream analyst notices bad numbers in a report, not through proactive monitoring.

Regulatory affairs teams then spend 6 months manually hunting compliance gaps, reconstructing audit trails, mapping CDISC standards, and assembling submission packages — entirely reactive, entirely preventable.

No autonomous solution exists today that monitors clinical trial data pipelines in real time, validates against FDA regulatory standards, auto-remediates issues with a compliant audit trail, and packages submission-ready data — without human intervention. The tools that exist (Monte Carlo, Datafold) alert. They do not act. They do not understand regulatory context. And none of them speak FDA.

This gap costs the pharmaceutical industry billions annually — and costs patients waiting for life-saving drugs months they cannot afford.


Our solution

NexClinicalMind is the world's first autonomous clinical trial data compliance agent — built on Google ADK, Gemini 2.0 Flash, CrewAI, and Model Context Protocol (MCP).

It deploys 5 specialised AI sub-agents working in concert across the entire trial data lifecycle:

SENTINEL monitors every clinical data pipeline in real time via MCP connections to Airflow, dbt, and Snowflake. Pipeline failures are detected and classified within 30 seconds of occurrence — powered by Gemini 2.0 Flash inference and Airflow's REST API. The industry average today: 4–6 hours.

GUARDIAN continuously validates clinical data against FDA CDISC SDTM/ADaM standards during processing not after. Every data transformation is checked in real time, catching compliance violations before they become submission blockers.

COUNSEL connects via MCP to live FDA and EMA regulatory guidance documents. When a violation is detected, it doesn't say "data quality issue" — it says "this violates FDA 21 CFR Part 11 Section 3.2 and must be remediated before submission filing." Actionable regulatory context, not error codes.

REMEDIATE autonomously fixes low-risk compliance issues with a full 21 CFR Part 11 audit trail logged to Google Cloud — every correction documented, timestamped, and regulator-ready. High-risk items are escalated with a pre-written remediation brief for human review.

PACKAGER generates submission-ready data packages autonomously once a trial's data passes all compliance checks — eliminating the manual assembly work that consumes months of regulatory affairs time.

The result:

Pipeline failures that once went undetected for 4–6 hours are now surfaced and classified in under 30 seconds. Root cause diagnosis that consumed 2–3 hours of manual engineering effort is resolved instantly and autonomously. Submission data preparation that took regulatory affairs teams 6 months is compressed to 6 weeks. Audit trails that were previously assembled by hand are now generated automatically and fully compliant with 21 CFR Part 11 from day one. And regulatory compliance visibility that was entirely retrospective — discovered only at submission time — becomes continuous and real-time throughout the entire trial lifecycle.

Submission preparation compresses from 6 months to 6 weeks — because NexClinicalMind eliminates the manual work that causes the delay: CDISC mapping is automated by GUARDIAN during processing, audit trails are built in real time by REMEDIATE, and data packaging is handled autonomously by PACKAGER. The 6 weeks that remain represent human review and final regulatory sign-off — work that genuinely requires human judgment.

NexClinicalMind is not a monitoring dashboard. It is the autonomous compliance layer that sits between pharma's data infrastructure and the FDA — a layer that today does not exist, costs the industry billions, and which NexInt AI is uniquely positioned to build.

Built on: Google ADK · Gemini 2.0 Flash · Model Context Protocol (MCP) · CrewAI · Google Cloud Run · GCP Cloud Logging (21 CFR Part 11 audit trail)

description_full: Problem to solveCost-effective vibe coding.Our solutionWe are building Kindred AI Studio, a coding studio that lets you build faster prototypes using Agent Swarm architecture and make super cost-effective token and model usage. This platform will be your personal assistant to build you a web app prototype of your dreams while remaining cheap. "How is it cost-effective?" It becomes cost-effective when it filters your prompt using Gemini and labels your prompt into three categories likeEasy—if the website is not a robust architecture like building a CRM or e-commerce platform or just a minor feature or bug in the code, it switches to a lighter model that is able to handle small tokens or small GPU usage.Hard - If the app is a platform with robust architecture and a well-defined prompt from the user but still has a vague idea, it will ask the user what is specifically needed inside the chat as radio buttons.Extra—If the app is a robust architecture-defined platform with a set of 1 million contexts buildable, which is determined by Gemini, then this app is a set of systems built into one.So the AI agent built within ADK, after getting past the prompt filter stage, researches, Google searches, and does necessary tool usage, including, if necessary, adding or calling MCP configured in the GCP Agent Platform connected to the app. When building now, it uses the Kimi AI architecture of agent swarm, running an AI agent with sub-agents building in the background, including an error-handling sub-agent, which is a critical feature of the platform for users to test error-free generated code.
description_html:
Problem to solve

Cost-effective vibe coding.

Our solution

We are building Kindred AI Studio, a coding studio that lets you build faster prototypes using Agent Swarm architecture and make super cost-effective token and model usage. This platform will be your personal assistant to build you a web app prototype of your dreams while remaining cheap. "How is it cost-effective?" It becomes cost-effective when it filters your prompt using Gemini and labels your prompt into three categories like

  • Easy—if the website is not a robust architecture like building a CRM or e-commerce platform or just a minor feature or bug in the code, it switches to a lighter model that is able to handle small tokens or small GPU usage.
  • Hard - If the app is a platform with robust architecture and a well-defined prompt from the user but still has a vague idea, it will ask the user what is specifically needed inside the chat as radio buttons.
  • Extra—If the app is a robust architecture-defined platform with a set of 1 million contexts buildable, which is determined by Gemini, then this app is a set of systems built into one.

So the AI agent built within ADK, after getting past the prompt filter stage, researches, Google searches, and does necessary tool usage, including, if necessary, adding or calling MCP configured in the GCP Agent Platform connected to the app. When building now, it uses the Kimi AI architecture of agent swarm, running an AI agent with sub-agents building in the background, including an error-handling sub-agent, which is a critical feature of the platform for users to test error-free generated code.

ARIA deploys 6 specialized sub-agents orchestrated by Google ADK:


- Intake Agent — document completeness verification across all 7 required KYC docs

- Guardian Agent — adaptive quality recovery, specific resubmission guidance (core innovation)

- Veritas Agent — cross-document intelligence, data extraction and consistency checking

- Compliance Agent — live MCP regulatory checks across 8 databases including FIU-IND and SEBI

- Risk Agent — 0-100 RAG scoring engine with pattern detection

- Report Agent — structured compliance output with officer instructions and Excel export


Human compliance officer makes every final decision. ARIA never approves autonomously.


ARIA operates as a closed-loop compliance system:


Document Intake → Quality Assessment → Data Extraction → Regulatory Check → Risk Scoring → Report Generation → Human Decision → Audit Log → Next Case


Unlike basic portals that stop at document collection, ARIA continues the loop — flagging patterns, detecting fraud networks, and improving officer efficiency with every case.

description_html:
Problem to solve

Healthcare consultations, particularly within telemedicine platforms and high-volume outpatient clinics, exhibit significant inefficiencies. Clinicians routinely allocate a substantial portion of encounter time to collecting basic patient history and symptom information, rather than focusing on diagnostic reasoning and therapeutic decision-making.


Patients often:


• Provide incomplete, ambiguous, or unstructured descriptions of symptoms


• Omit critical clinical details unless systematically prompted


• Lack mechanisms to transmit objective physiological data (e.g., vitals) during remote consultations


These limitations contribute to:


• Prolonged consultation durations


• Variability and gaps in clinical documentation


• Delays in triage and prioritization of care


• Reduced scalability and efficiency of healthcare delivery systems, particularly in resource-constrained settings


In remote or rural contexts, these challenges are further exacerbated; clinical care frequently cannot be initiated until a physician is directly available, resulting in preventable delays and potentially adverse health outcomes.

Our solution

1st Doctor – AI-powered pre‑consultation for healthcare


1st Doctor is an AI-powered pre-consultation system that structures a patient’s case before the doctor interaction begins. Instead of a simple chatbot, it’s a multi-agent clinical workflow that automates and upgrades intake.


What it does


• Dynamic intake: Asks adaptive follow-up questions based on patient responses to capture precise symptom history.


• Multimodal understanding: Processes camera-based vitals (heart rate, HRV, SpO₂) and respiratory audio alongside text.


• Grounded medical reasoning: Uses hybrid retrieval (BM25 + vector search) over medical knowledge to keep suggestions clinically grounded.


• Structured clinical summary: Synthesizes all inputs into a concise, structured report with possible conditions and precautions for the clinician.


How it works (AI + agents) Built with a modular multi-agent architecture (LangGraph):


• Intake Agent – collects, clarifies, and refines symptoms.


• Vitals Agent – turns raw sensor data into clinically meaningful signals.


• Clinical Reasoning Agent – combines all inputs into a structured, doctor-ready summary.


Why it matters


• Reduces intake time for doctors and staff.


• Improves completeness and consistency of patient data.


• Enables earlier triage even before a doctor is available.


1st Doctor acts as an intelligent intake layer for healthcare, so clinicians start with structured, data-rich cases instead of raw, messy conversations, unlocking safer, faster, and more scalable care.

project_name: Tindernát | AI Roommate Matching Platform for Universities
description_full: Problem to solveUniversities in Central and Eastern Europe assign student roommates manually using spreadsheets, with zero input from students about lifestyle compatibility. This leads to preventable conflicts — students with opposite sleep schedules, cleanliness habits, or study preferences end up sharing rooms, causing stress, academic underperformance, and costly administrative room-change requests.Our solutionTindernát is a B2B SaaS roommate-matching platform built for universities. We're building an AI agent on Google's Agent Development Kit (ADK) that collects structured lifestyle data from students via a smart questionnaire, runs a weighted compatibility algorithm to generate optimal roommate pairings, and gives housing administrators a dashboard to review, override, and publish assignments. The agent uses MCP to connect to university student data systems, automates the entire matching workflow, and makes the experience feel dynamic rather than static — surfacing explanations for each match and enabling pre-arrival student interaction.

description_html:
https://www.hudood.com/


Hudood Social is an evolution of the Hudood investment platform, transforming it into a safe, Shariah-compliant social ecosystem for Muslim youth. By leveraging Google Cloud Vertex AI and Gemini, we are building a platform where social interaction is moderated by AI to ensure religious and financial integrity, and investment decisions are guided by agents grounded in deep Shariah screening data.


 The Problem

  1. Market Fragmentation: Muslim Gen-Z and Millennials are forced to discuss finance on generic platforms (Instagram/Twitter) where content is often "haram" or unmoderated.
  2. Analysis Paralysis: Shariah-compliant investing is complex. Users struggle to understand why a stock is halal or haram based on various methodologies (Itqan, Ehtiyat, Taqwa).
  3. Safety & Trust: Financial communities require high-standard moderation to prevent misinformation, scams, and non-compliant social behavior.



💡 The Solution: Hudood Social

We are implementing three specialized Google Cloud AI Agents to solve these problems:

1. The Shariah Guardian (Moderation Agent)

  • Tech: Vertex AI Multi-modal Gemini.
  • Function: Automatically monitors the community feed to ensure all shared text and images align with Shariah-compliant community standards (modesty, ethical speech, etc.).

2. The Investment Mufti (Knowledge Agent)

  • Tech: Gemini + Vertex AI Search (RAG).
  • Function: Conversational access to our proprietary database of 10,000+ screened stocks. Users can ask: "Why did Hudood flag this crypto as haram?" and get an explanation grounded in the three screening methodologies.

3. The Wealth Wellness Coach (Portfolio Agent)

  • Tech: Gemini Pro.
  • Function: Analyzes user portfolio and Zakaat data to provide personalized spiritual-financial advice, such as identifying purification amounts for "borderline" halal stocks.

Yaddai’s goal is to commercialize specialized creative agents directly to users, helping creators and businesses plan, evaluate, improve, and produce media with increasing reliability.

description_full: FastCVThe agentic application engine for job seekersPitch Document · v1.0 · April 2026Executive Summary:FastCV turns one master profile into a fully tailored, role-specific resume and cover letter in under 60 seconds. Built on Anthropic's Model Context Protocol (MCP) and Google's Agent2Agent (A2A) protocol, FastCV orchestrates a network of specialised AI agents - a JD analyser, a profile matcher, a resume rewriter, and a cover letter writer - that collaborate to produce documents that read like they were written for that one job, because they were. The market is ready: software engineering job postings are up 11% year-over-year, but entry-level hiring is down 73% and the average corporate role attracts hundreds of generic applications. Generic no longer works. FastCV is how you stop being generic.The problemThe 2026 job market punishes generic applicants. Hiring has rebounded - software engineer postings rose roughly 11% year-over-year per Citadel Securities, and AI/ML hiring grew 88% in 2025 - but the bar moved with it. Companies have shifted from growth-at-all-costs hiring to precision hiring: smaller teams, higher expectations, ATS-driven first-pass filtering, and a 73% drop in entry-level hiring rates. The candidate pool per role has grown while the recruiter attention per applicant has shrunk to seconds.Job-specific applications are now table stakes. Every recruiter survey, every ATS vendor, and every hiring manager's LinkedIn post says the same thing: tailored resumes get interviews, and generic resumes get rejected. description_html:
FastCV

The agentic application engine for job seekers

Pitch Document · v1.0 · April 2026


Executive Summary:

FastCV turns one master profile into a fully tailored, role-specific resume and cover letter in under 60 seconds. Built on Anthropic's Model Context Protocol (MCP) and Google's Agent2Agent (A2A) protocol, FastCV orchestrates a network of specialised AI agents - a JD analyser, a profile matcher, a resume rewriter, and a cover letter writer - that collaborate to produce documents that read like they were written for that one job, because they were. The market is ready: software engineering job postings are up 11% year-over-year, but entry-level hiring is down 73% and the average corporate role attracts hundreds of generic applications. Generic no longer works. FastCV is how you stop being generic.

The problem

The 2026 job market punishes generic applicants. Hiring has rebounded - software engineer postings rose roughly 11% year-over-year per Citadel Securities, and AI/ML hiring grew 88% in 2025 - but the bar moved with it. Companies have shifted from growth-at-all-costs hiring to precision hiring: smaller teams, higher expectations, ATS-driven first-pass filtering, and a 73% drop in entry-level hiring rates. The candidate pool per role has grown while the recruiter attention per applicant has shrunk to seconds.

Job-specific applications are now table stakes. Every recruiter survey, every ATS vendor, and every hiring manager's LinkedIn post says the same thing: tailored resumes get interviews, and generic resumes get rejected. But tailoring takes 45–90 minutes per application - reading the JD, matching keywords, rewriting bullets, and drafting a cover letter - and most candidates apply to 50+ roles. The math doesn't work.

What candidates do today (and why it fails)

  • Send the same resume everywhere. Fast, but ATS keyword filters and human screeners reject it within seconds.
  • Manually tweak each application. Effective, but unsustainable past 5–10 applications. Burnout sets in, quality drops, applications stop.
  • Use a generic ChatGPT prompt. Produces fluent text that sounds AI-written, lacks measurable impact, doesn't map to the candidate's real evidence, and reads identical to every other AI-assisted application in the pile.
  • Pay a resume writer ($200–$800 per package). High quality but slow turnaround (3–7 days) and economically unviable for the 50+ roles a job seeker actually targets.

The gap FastCV fills

There is no tool today that combines (a) a structured, machine-readable record of a candidate's full career evidence; (b) deep semantic understanding of a specific job description; and (c) collaborative AI agents that produce role-specific documents at scale - fast enough to apply to 20 jobs in an evening and accurate enough that each one reads like it was hand-crafted.

The FastCV Solution

One profile in. Tailored applications are out. A candidate uploads their CV and answers a short structured intake (projects, metrics, achievements, preferences). FastCV converts this into a canonical career graph - a structured, queryable record of every role, project, technology, and quantified outcome. From that single source of truth, every future application is generated.

The user flow

  1. Paste a job description (URL or text). FastCV extracts requirements, must-haves, nice-to-haves, company signals, and tone.
  2. Match. FastCV scores the candidate's career graph against the JD, surfaces the strongest evidence, and flags genuine gaps the candidate should know about.
  3. Generate. Specialised agents collaborate via A2A to produce a role-tuned resume (ATS-optimised) and a cover letter that opens with what the candidate will deliver in 3–6 months - not 'I am passionate.'
  4. Refine. Inline edit, regenerate sections, change tone (confident/measured/direct), or pivot the angle. Every change is reflected back into the canonical profile so the system gets better with each use.
  5. Export: PDF, DOCX, plain text for ATS forms, and a one-tap copy for LinkedIn Easy Apply.

Architecture: why MCP + A2A is the unlock


FastCV is built as a multi-agent system. Each agent owns one job and does it well. The agents communicate using Google A2A (agent-to-agent), and they reach the outside world - job boards, ATS systems, and the candidate's Drive - through Anthropic MCP (agent-to-tool). This separation isn't decorative; it's what lets FastCV swap models, scale agents independently, and integrate with any platform a candidate already uses.

Agents in the FastCV mesh

Why FastCV wins

The Inversion

The job application has been broken for thirty years: a static document trying to represent a dynamic career in a market that rewards specificity and punishes generality. Every fix - templates, keyword tools, AI rewriters - polished the resume. FastCV rejects that. The candidate's evidence is the source of truth; the resume is a disposable artifact, regenerated per role in 60 seconds. Built on open agent standards (MCP and A2A), FastCV is how professionals apply now.

description_html:
Problem to solve

Founders, creators, and small marketing teams are drowning in the daily grind of social media. Posting consistently on LinkedIn, X, Instagram, TikTok, Threads, Facebook, and YouTube Shorts is a full-time job: every channel needs a different tone, format, length, hook, hashtag strategy, and posting cadence. Most teams either burn out, post low-quality content, hire expensive agencies, or simply go silent — losing reach, leads, and brand momentum.


Our solution

WeAreCentaurs is a swarm of specialized AI agents that plan, write, design, schedule, and publish social media content end-to-end across all major platforms — autonomously, on-brand, and 24/7. Instead of one generic "AI poster", we orchestrate a team of agents where each one is an expert at a single job, coordinated by a central planner agent. Humans stay in the loop only when they want to, with one-click approvals from a unified dashboard or directly from Slack/WhatsApp.


How it works

A user connects their brand (website, past posts, tone-of-voice doc, product catalog) and their social accounts. From that moment, the swarm takes over:


  1. Strategist Agent — analyzes the brand, competitors, trending topics on each platform, and the content calendar to decide WHAT to post, WHEN, and WHY.
  2. 2. Researcher Agent — pulls fresh data: news, Reddit threads, YouTube transcripts, podcast clips, internal CRM events, product updates, and turns them into raw content briefs.
  3. 3. Copywriter Agent — adapts every brief into platform-native copy: long-form for LinkedIn, punchy threads for X, captions for Instagram, scripts for TikTok/Reels/Shorts, and SEO-friendly titles for YouTube.
  4. 4. Designer Agent — generates carousels, quote cards, memes, and thumbnails using Imagen and Nano Banana, always respecting brand colors, fonts, and visual identity.
  5. 5. Video Agent — produces short vertical videos with Veo, including B-roll, captions, and voice-over via Chirp, plus auto-cut highlights from long-form content.
  6. 6. Scheduler Agent — picks the optimal posting time per platform per audience timezone, avoiding overlap and respecting rate limits.
  7. 7. Publisher Agent — pushes content to LinkedIn, X, Instagram, TikTok, Threads, Facebook, YouTube Shorts, Pinterest, and Bluesky through their official APIs.
  8. 8. Community Agent — monitors comments and DMs, drafts on-brand replies, escalates leads to the user, and reports sentiment in real time.
  9. 9. Analyst Agent — measures performance, runs A/B tests on hooks and thumbnails, and feeds learnings back to the Strategist so the swarm gets smarter every week.

A central Orchestrator Agent (built on Google Agent Development Kit) routes tasks, handles retries, enforces brand guardrails, and exposes a single conversational interface where the user can simply say "Push a campaign for our Black Friday launch" and watch the agents collaborate.


Tech stack

  • Google Agent Development Kit (ADK) for multi-agent orchestration and tool use
  • - Gemini 2.5 Pro for reasoning, planning and copywriting
  • - Gemini Flash for high-volume, low-latency tasks (replies, classification, moderation)
  • - Imagen 4 and Nano Banana for image generation and editing
  • - Veo 3 for short-form video generation
  • - Chirp for multilingual voice-over
  • - Vertex AI Search + RAG over the brand knowledge base (past posts, brand book, product docs)
  • - Cloud Run + Pub/Sub for the event-driven backbone of the swarm
  • - Firestore for state, BigQuery for analytics, Secret Manager for social tokens
  • - Next.js dashboard on Firebase Hosting, with Slack and WhatsApp approval bots

What makes it different

Unlike Buffer, Hootsuite, or single-prompt "AI writers", WeAreCentaurs is not a tool — it is an autonomous social media team. It thinks in campaigns, not in isolated posts; it learns the brand voice instead of imitating it once; and it closes the loop between research, creation, publishing, and analytics without human handoffs.


Impact

Early pilots show 10x more posts published per week, 3x engagement, and ~85% reduction in time spent on social by founding teams — letting humans focus on strategy and product while the centaurs handle the feed.


Next steps

Open beta in June, native integrations with HubSpot and Shopify, and a marketplace where brands can plug in their own specialized agents (e.g., a "legal review" agent for regulated industries).

description_html:
Problem to solve


Road infrastructure deteriorates over time due to traffic load, weather conditions, and poor maintenance. This leads to multiple types of road distress such as potholes, cracks, surface wear, edge failures, and uneven pavements.


Today, most road inspections are manual teams physically survey roads, which is time-consuming, expensive, and often inconsistent. As a result:


  • Damage is detected late
  • Severity is not accurately assessed
  • Maintenance decisions are delayed


This directly impacts public safety, increases vehicle damage, and raises long-term repair costs. There is a clear need for a faster, more reliable, and scalable way to monitor road conditions.


Our solution


We are building an AI-powered Road Intelligence Agent that automates road condition monitoring and analysis.


The system uses computer vision and intelligent agents to:


  • Detect multiple types of road distress (potholes, cracks, surface degradation, uneven roads) using a YOLOv8 model
  • Analyze the severity of each issue (low, medium, high) based on visual patterns
  • Provide actionable recommendations for maintenance (e.g., patching, resurfacing, urgent repair)
  • Generate structured reports that can assist authorities in prioritizing repairs


By combining machine learning with an agent-based approach, the system not only identifies problems but also interprets them and suggests next steps. This makes road maintenance more proactive, efficient, and scalable.

project_name: IntervYOU - Practice Mock Interviews with AI
description_full: Ready to start building? Check out this guide for next steps.Problem to solveThe biggest lie in tech recruitment is "If you can code, you will clear the interview." Most early-career developers possess strong technical skills but freeze during high-pressure, live technical interviews. Existing solutions like practicing with text-based LLMs (like ChatGPT) are ineffective because they are "too polite" and do not simulate the brutal, time-constrained reality of a voice-to-voice interview. Furthermore, human mock interviews are expensive and inaccessible for the masses.Our solutionWe are building IntervYOU, a strictly Voice-to-Voice AI Interviewer Agent. For this "Optimize" track, we are upgrading our existing prototype into a fully autonomous agent using Gemini. The agent doesn't just chat; it ingests a user's Resume and Job Description, dynamically generates technical cross-questions, and uses function calling to verify the candidate's technical claims. It interrupts candidates, handles hesitation, and ultimately generates a brutal, analytics-driven scorecard on both technical accuracy and communication confidence.

description_full: Ready to start building? Check out this guide for next steps.Problem to solveB2B sellers can’t tell which prospects are actually evaluating vs. politely browsing. Inbound forms are dying, cold outbound is interrupted and ignored, and third-party intent data is anonymous, account-level, and weeks late. Reps waste hours chasing the wrong accounts and miss the small handful of buyers who were ready to talk this week. The signals already exist - in the conversations buyers have when they research a vendor - but no tool captures them at the rep level, in real time, with named identities attached.Our solutionParsley is the digital business card that reveals buyer intent. Each seller gets a personal profile they share in DMs, email signatures, and QR codes. On that profile sits an AI assistant grounded in the seller’s own product docs, ready to answer buyer questions in natural conversation. As the buyer chats, we run topic modeling over their questions and map them to a taxonomy of buyer-intent themes (pricing, integrations, security, competitive comparison, implementation, ROI, and so on), plus a free-text theme for anything new the taxonomy hasn’t seen yet. Each conversation produces a named contact, a topic profile, and a Hot/Warm/Cold score, all synced into the seller’s CRM as structured fields and tags. The result: first-party, named, topic-level intent data delivered the moment a buyer self-identifies.

project_name: ADV Strategy Pro: Sovereign Multi-Agent AEO Infrastructure
description_full: Problem to solve. Digital visibility is shifting from "ten blue links" to AI Overviews and agentic discovery engines. Most business data is semantically invisible to LLMs because it lacks structured grounding, JSON-LD typing, and citation density. Existing AI-content stacks are fragmented and hallucination-prone, while mid-market companies pay agencies $5K–$15K/month for template-quality work in 4–8 weeks. The bottleneck isn't generation — it's coordination, grounding, and fiscal discipline at the agent layer.Our solution. ADV Strategy Pro is a production-grade multi-agent ecosystem built natively on the Google Agent Development Kit (ADK 1.23+), the open A2A protocol, Vertex AI (Gemini 3.1 Pro / Flash-Lite, Imagen 4, Nano Banana, Veo), and the Gemini Live API. It runs an 8-wave AEO pipeline with 13 critical-path agents: Research → ICP → Campaign → Content → SEO → UX/Visual → Experience/Presentation → Deployment → Analytics.The architectural moat is a first-class A2A Agent Registry implemented on the ADK canon. Every agent extends BaseCoreAgent(LlmAgent) and ships an A2A-canonical AgentCard declaring produces / consumes / actions / tools / tier_pin / cost_envelope. The registry has three primitives: a 325-LOC Pydantic v2 schema, a 365-LOC DiscoveryService with first-write-wins name-level dedup, and a 211-LOC inverted CapabilityIndex for O(1) capability → agent lookups. 44 AgentCards live on disk (13 production tier-1 + 24 auto-generated tier-2 + 4 curated tier-2 + 3 in agency hub). @adk_tool decorators feed AgentCard.tools so the registry stays in sync with code by construction.Pipelines run as a Pydantic v2 PipelineManifest DAG with stable digest, executed by a 1,436-LOC DurableExecutor — resumable, idempotent, recoverable. A Director factory routes to either a deterministic LLM-free planner or an LLM planner with NFKC-hardened Sandwich Defense (preserves brand glyphs ™/½/ⅠⅡⅢ byte-for-byte). HITL approval rides a digest-bound queue with cross-process advisory lock; SpendLedger reservations get a daemon heartbeat() that auto-refreshes long-running nodes (>10 min).Memory has two tiers. Hippocampus (ChromaDB, per-agent vectors, client-scoped via client_id injection) feeds prior work into the LLM system prompt before generation. Grimoire (Supabase pgvector) stores brand voice, pricing, prior campaigns. Memory Bank Phase 3.2 ships LocalAiSessionService with asyncio.Lock thread-safety, cross-tenant namespacing, bounded MAX_EVENTS, and MEMORY_BANK_FALLBACK=strict.Output quality is enforced by 4 pure-function post-flight validators on every run: AeoOutputValidator (≥70/100), ConversionValidator (4 categories), PricingCanonValidator (non_canonical_literal ERROR with survey-context suppression), GoldStandardValidator (8-feature structural similarity vs goldens). 5-Layer Cost Defense (Research Lock + Intent Classifier + GroundingGuard + 5-query cap + SpendGate) plus a GCS semantic execute-cache and ContextPruner on every LLM call deliver an order-of-magnitude reduction in per-run spend.Production reliability (verified live): 6 Cloud Run services in us-central1, Memorystore Redis event bus across instances via VPC connector, 10 internal MCP servers over a unified ADK-native MCPToolset (shared 252-LOC stdio JSON-RPC module with auth fail-loud), Stripe webhook with signature verification, RevenueGate freeze-not-die. 7,976 automated tests on main, 162K LOC Python, automated release: published deploy with /health smoke gate. 5/5 green mock dry rehearsal of the full chain at $0.Business case. ADV is the integrator that connects marketing spend, sales pipeline, and P&L margin into one operator workflow — built on a financial model, not a content calendar. Pricing canon (Stripe-live, March 2026): free $0 AEO Live Audit (lead capture) → $197 AEO Intelligence Report → $697 Protocol-Ready Landing Page → $2,497 Multi-Page Semantic Site → $4,997 Scale Strategic Site → $10,000+ Enterprise Custom. Commercial centre of gravity is monthly retainers — $1,497/mo AIO Maintenance, $1,497/mo Google Ads Management, $1,997/mo Growth Bundle (primary recurring-revenue target). The Voice layer (VCMO + Voice Closer on Gemini Live API) ships as a production capability inside the retainer, not a separate SKU. Founder dogfood live at adv-it-performance.ca. ADV IT Performance Corp is a certified Google Partner since 2020.═══════════════════════════════════════════════════Access to the code repository═══════════════════════════════════════════════════The repository is private during the hackathon judging period for security reasons (commit history may contain references to internal infrastructure). Judges can request read access by emailing daniil.agarev@gmail.com with their GitHub username. Access is granted within hours, typically minutes.If access is urgent during evaluation, please mention "Antigravity Hackathon Judge" in the email subject for prioritized response.═══════════════════════════════════════════════════

The bottleneck isn't generation — it's coordination, grounding, and fiscal discipline at the agent layer.


Our solution. ADV Strategy Pro is a production-grade multi-agent ecosystem built natively on the Google Agent Development Kit (ADK 1.23+), the open A2A protocol, Vertex AI (Gemini 3.1 Pro / Flash-Lite, Imagen 4, Nano Banana, Veo), and the Gemini Live API. It runs an 8-wave AEO pipeline with 13 critical-path agents: Research → ICP → Campaign → Content → SEO → UX/Visual → Experience/Presentation → Deployment → Analytics.


The architectural moat is a first-class A2A Agent Registry implemented on the ADK canon. Every agent extends BaseCoreAgent(LlmAgent) and ships an A2A-canonical AgentCard declaring produces / consumes / actions / tools / tier_pin / cost_envelope. The registry has three primitives: a 325-LOC Pydantic v2 schema, a 365-LOC DiscoveryService with first-write-wins name-level dedup, and a 211-LOC inverted CapabilityIndex for O(1) capability → agent lookups. 44 AgentCards live on disk (13 production tier-1 + 24 auto-generated tier-2 + 4 curated tier-2 + 3 in agency hub). @adk_tool decorators feed AgentCard.tools so the registry stays in sync with code by construction.


Pipelines run as a Pydantic v2 PipelineManifest DAG with stable digest, executed by a 1,436-LOC DurableExecutor — resumable, idempotent, recoverable. A Director factory routes to either a deterministic LLM-free planner or an LLM planner with NFKC-hardened Sandwich Defense (preserves brand glyphs ™/½/ⅠⅡⅢ byte-for-byte). HITL approval rides a digest-bound queue with cross-process advisory lock; SpendLedger reservations get a daemon heartbeat() that auto-refreshes long-running nodes (>10 min).


Memory has two tiers. Hippocampus (ChromaDB, per-agent vectors, client-scoped via client_id injection) feeds prior work into the LLM system prompt before generation. Grimoire (Supabase pgvector) stores brand voice, pricing, prior campaigns. Memory Bank Phase 3.2 ships LocalAiSessionService with asyncio.Lock thread-safety, cross-tenant namespacing, bounded MAX_EVENTS, and MEMORY_BANK_FALLBACK=strict.


Output quality is enforced by 4 pure-function post-flight validators on every run: AeoOutputValidator (≥70/100), ConversionValidator (4 categories), PricingCanonValidator (non_canonical_literal ERROR with survey-context suppression), GoldStandardValidator (8-feature structural similarity vs goldens). 5-Layer Cost Defense (Research Lock + Intent Classifier + GroundingGuard + 5-query cap + SpendGate) plus a GCS semantic execute-cache and ContextPruner on every LLM call deliver an order-of-magnitude reduction in per-run spend.

Production reliability (verified live): 6 Cloud Run services in us-central1, Memorystore Redis event bus across instances via VPC connector, 10 internal MCP servers over a unified ADK-native MCPToolset (shared 252-LOC stdio JSON-RPC module with auth fail-loud), Stripe webhook with signature verification, RevenueGate freeze-not-die. 7,976 automated tests on main, 162K LOC Python, automated release: published deploy with /health smoke gate. 5/5 green mock dry rehearsal of the full chain at $0.


Business case. ADV is the integrator that connects marketing spend, sales pipeline, and P&L margin into one operator workflow — built on a financial model, not a content calendar. Pricing canon (Stripe-live, March 2026): free $0 AEO Live Audit (lead capture) → $197 AEO Intelligence Report → $697 Protocol-Ready Landing Page → $2,497 Multi-Page Semantic Site → $4,997 Scale Strategic Site → $10,000+ Enterprise Custom. Commercial centre of gravity is monthly retainers — $1,497/mo AIO Maintenance, $1,497/mo Google Ads Management, $1,997/mo Growth Bundle (primary recurring-revenue target). The Voice layer (VCMO + Voice Closer on Gemini Live API) ships as a production capability inside the retainer, not a separate SKU. Founder dogfood live at adv-it-performance.ca. ADV IT Performance Corp is a certified Google Partner since 2020.

description_html:
Glassbox Engine

Problem

Machine learning personalization still suffers from a “black box” problem. As models become more powerful, they often become harder for developers, businesses, and end-users to understand.


This creates three practical failures:


The Alignment Gap: Businesses want recommendations that balance goals such as revenue, discovery, user trust, and margin. But most ML systems optimize a single technical objective, such as clicks or conversions, which can conflict with long-term brand value.


The Knowledge Gap: New ML engineers increasingly rely on generated training scripts without fully understanding the math, tradeoffs, or failure modes behind them. This leads to fragile models that are difficult to debug, tune, or trust in production.


The Trust Gap: End-users are becoming more skeptical of AI-driven recommendations. When a system cannot explain why something was recommended, users may feel manipulated instead of supported.


Today, there is no simple platform that combines ML education, business-goal alignment, simulation, and explainable production recommendations in one workflow.

Our solution


We are building GlassBox Engine, an agent-driven ML platform that makes personalization transparent, testable, and aligned with business goals.


Built with Google’s Agent Development Kit and Gemini, GlassBox Engine uses a multi-agent architecture to support the ML lifecycle from objective design to simulation, training, and explainable recommendation delivery. ADK is a strong fit because it is designed for building, debugging, deploying, and orchestrating multi-agent systems.


The platform has four core components:


Intent Alignment Sliders

Business users define goals such as Profit, Discovery, Trust, and Diversity through a visual interface. The Architect Agent converts these preferences into a transparent reward-function configuration that developers can inspect and adjust.


The Socratic Mentor

An agent-integrated coding environment helps ML engineers understand what they are building. Instead of simply autocompleting code, the Mentor Agent asks targeted questions about loss functions, model layers, evaluation metrics, and data assumptions.


The Persona Lab

The Coordinator Agent generates synthetic user personas and simulated behavior scenarios to test how recommendation strategies perform under different user segments, preference shifts, and data-drift conditions before deployment.


The GlassBox Plugin

A pluggable tRPC API serves recommendations from a PostgreSQL and pgvector backend. Each recommendation includes a human-readable decision trace explaining which user signals, product attributes, and business constraints contributed to the result.


By combining agentic collaboration, explainable reward design, and simulated testing, GlassBox Engine helps teams build personalization systems that are not only effective, but understandable and accountable.

description_html:
Ready to start building? Check out this guide for next steps.

Problem to solve


The "Multi-Cloud Blind Spot" and Execution Gap.

Startups and enterprises using multi-cloud environments (AWS, GCP, Azure) face three critical challenges that current FinOps dashboards fail to solve:

  • Data Fragmentation: Cost data is siloed. A spike in AWS might be related to a data transfer in GCP, but connecting these dots manually takes hours of engineering time.
  • The "Notification Fatigue" Execution Gap: Traditional tools send alerts (e.g., "Your S3 costs rose 20%"), but they don't act. Busy DevOps teams often ignore these alerts until the end of the billing cycle, leading to "bill shock."
  • Contextual Complexity: Not all spikes are "bad." Differentiating between a revenue-generating traffic surge and a "zombie" resource (like an unattached 10TB EBS volume) requires deep contextual knowledge of the infrastructure that static rules cannot provide.

Our solution


Costmatic AI: An Autonomous Multi-Cloud FinOps Orchestrator.

We are building a production-grade AI Agent using the Google Agent Development Kit (ADK) and Gemini 3.1 that transforms FinOps from a monitoring task into an autonomous service.

Key Features:

  • Declarative Cost Governance: Instead of setting manual thresholds, users define intent (e.g., "Keep my dev environment under $500/mo"). The agent autonomously manages resource states to meet this goal.
  • Cross-Cloud Reasoning via MCP: Leveraging the Model Context Protocol (MCP), the agent connects directly to our MongoDB "Source of Truth" and live Cloud APIs. It can "read" a spike in one cloud and "investigate" the root cause in another.
  • Autonomous Optimization (Root Cause to Resolution):
  • Detect: Identifies anomalies in real-time.
  • Diagnose: Uses Gemini’s multi-step reasoning to determine if a resource is "zombie," over-provisioned, or orphaned.
  • Execute: Generates and—upon human-in-the-loop approval via ADK’s ToolConfirmation—executes CLI commands or Terraform scripts to resize, stop, or delete wasteful resources.
  • Predictive Refactoring: The agent analyzes historical MongoDB data to propose "Commitment-Based Discounts" (like GCP Sustained Use or AWS RI) and generates the business case for the CFO automatically.

project_name: Column: AI Agent That Builds MedusaJS Ecommerce Stores
description_full: Problem to solveBuilding a custom ecommerce website is still too slow and technical for many founders, creators, and small brands. Existing website builders are easy to use but often lock merchants into rigid templates, platform fees, and limited code ownership. On the other side, developer-first commerce stacks like MedusaJS and Next.js give full control, but require backend setup, frontend engineering, product modeling, styling, debugging, and deployment knowledge.The problem Column solves is the gap between “I know what store I want” and “I have a working ecommerce codebase.” A non-technical founder should be able to describe their store idea in plain English and get a functional ecommerce app instead of starting from a blank repo or a generic template.Our solutionColumn is a web-based autonomous ecommerce-building agent. A user describes the ecommerce website they want, and Column boots a prepared development sandbox with MedusaJS, Next.js, Node.js, Tailwind CSS, and required ecommerce dependencies already installed.Inside that sandbox, the agent works like an autonomous developer: it reads the existing project structure, edits files, runs commands, checks errors, seeds products, and produces a working ecommerce storefront. For the challenge MVP, Column will focus on generating a functional Medusa-powered ecommerce app from a natural language brief, including a storefront, product data, product listing pages, product detail pages, and cart flow.The goal is not to be a generic AI website builder. Column is specialized for ecommerce, where the agent starts from a real commerce stack instead of generating only static pages.

Problem to solve

Building a custom ecommerce website is still too slow and technical for many founders, creators, and small brands. Existing website builders are easy to use but often lock merchants into rigid templates, platform fees, and limited code ownership. On the other side, developer-first commerce stacks like MedusaJS and Next.js give full control, but require backend setup, frontend engineering, product modeling, styling, debugging, and deployment knowledge.


The problem Column solves is the gap between “I know what store I want” and “I have a working ecommerce codebase.” A non-technical founder should be able to describe their store idea in plain English and get a functional ecommerce app instead of starting from a blank repo or a generic template.

Our solution

Column is a web-based autonomous ecommerce-building agent. A user describes the ecommerce website they want, and Column boots a prepared development sandbox with MedusaJS, Next.js, Node.js, Tailwind CSS, and required ecommerce dependencies already installed.


Inside that sandbox, the agent works like an autonomous developer: it reads the existing project structure, edits files, runs commands, checks errors, seeds products, and produces a working ecommerce storefront. For the challenge MVP, Column will focus on generating a functional Medusa-powered ecommerce app from a natural language brief, including a storefront, product data, product listing pages, product detail pages, and cart flow.


The goal is not to be a generic AI website builder. Column is specialized for ecommerce, where the agent starts from a real commerce stack instead of generating only static pages.

description_html:
Problem to solve

Designing scalable, production-ready systems is complex, time-consuming, and requires expertise across system architecture, infrastructure, and DevOps.


For startups and developers, moving from an idea to a deployable system often takes days or weeks. This slows down innovation, increases engineering costs, and creates a barrier for non-experts trying to build reliable systems.


Existing tools typically stop at diagrams or documentation, leaving a large gap between system design and real-world implementation.

Our solution

Emkraws AI is an autonomous AI agent that transforms a simple natural language prompt into a complete, production-ready system architecture.


With a single prompt, the system:

  • Generates architecture diagrams using Mermaid
  • Breaks systems into scalable monolith, microservices etc
  • Recommends production-ready tech stacks
  • Produces infrastructure-as-code using Terraform
  • Generates Docker configurations for containerization
  • Creates CI/CD pipelines for automated deployment

Additionally, the system includes a deployment workflow that can initiate or simulate execution, demonstrating how generated systems move from design to real-world readiness.


AI goes beyond traditional design tools by not only describing systems, but preparing them for execution.


It bridges the gap between:



Idea → Architecture → Infrastructure → Deployment



This transforms system design from a static process into an actionable, end-to-end workflow. The system is model-agnostic and can integrate with different LLM providers depending on requirements.


Technical Implementation

  • Backend: FastAPI (Python)
  • AI Layer: LLM-powered agent workflow (Gemini)
  • Infrastructure Generation: Terraform
  • Containerization: Docker
  • CI/CD: GitHub Actions
  • Deployment: Google cloud run (backend), Vercel (frontend)

The system follows an agent-based approach that:

  • Interprets user intent
  • Decomposes systems into services
  • Generates deployable assets
  • Supports execution workflows

Business Impact

Emkraws AI significantly reduces the time required to design and prepare production systems—from weeks to minutes.


It enables:

  • Faster MVP development
  • Reduced DevOps overhead
  • Accessibility for non-expert builders
  • Improved engineering productivity

Use Cases

  • Startups building scalable MVPs
  • Developers designing distributed systems
  • Teams accelerating architecture decisions

Future Work

  • Full automated cloud deployment
  • Cost optimization and architecture scoring
  • Real-time monitoring and feedback loops

description_full: QLD2032 is a System of Action for Queensland construction procurement intelligence — a vertical-specific implementation of the Agentic Data Cloud pattern Google announced at Cloud Next 2026, built independently by a solo founder. Domain registered 2019, dormant 2021-2025, active build commenced November 2025. The platform's first four months were built on a Samsung S23 Ultra phone between first-aid kit servicing jobs across Gold Coast and Sunshine Coast worksites: AI-generated code copy-pasted into WordPress custom HTML blocks and HFCM (Header Footer Code Manager) snippets, validated by a manual AI Council (Claude reasoning + Gemini grounding + GPT review + Perplexity fact-checking) where only consensus items shipped. Track 2 — hardening this existing prototype for production-grade reliability.### Problem to solveQueensland's construction supply chain practitioners — estimators, project managers, tier-1 builders, subcontractors — operate across 35+ fragmented Queensland government data sources with no consolidated real-time intelligence layer. Procurement portals, council development pipelines, contractor licence registers, compliance databases, tender feeds, valuation data: each has its own schema, cadence, and silent failure mode (a recent example: a Queensland tender feed silently changed its status enum and froze data for 27 days before observation). Practitioners either build ad-hoc manual tracking or operate blind to upcoming procurement, contractor qualification status, and compliance changes.### Our solutionQLD2032 is structured in four architectural layers, mapping directly to the Agentic Data Cloud pattern:**Substrate brain.** A 113M-row BigQuery brain (11 GB across 64 tables) cross-referencing the full Australian Business Number register against Queensland-specific layers — 3.5M G-NAF addresses, 3.5M DCDB cadastral lots, 1.8M Valuer-General records, 530K historical procurement contracts, 196K QBCC-licensed contractors, and 9,824 first-seen council development applications across 13 councils. A Gemini File Search store grounds agents in a 78-file platform decision archive and (in active build) dark-data sources: regulatory PDFs, council DA documents, tender attachments, industry references.**AI Council orchestration.** Multi-model agents specialised by what each is reliably good at — Claude Opus 4.7 for reasoning and synthesis, Gemini for grounding and spatial queries, Sonnet 4.6 for high-throughput drift detection. All agents query the same shared substrate via a uniform tool interface. Migration to ADK on Gemini Enterprise Agent Platform underway for Chef (substrate ingestion), Brief Writer v3 (citation-grounded procurement intelligence), and Watcher (drift detection). The manual phone-based AI Council that bootstrapped the platform now has a managed migration path on Google's just-released BYO-MCP and Model Garden infrastructure.**MCP tool/action layer.** An OAuth 2.1-protected MCP server (hAPI) running as a hardened systemd service on a single GCP Compute Engine VM. BYO-MCP compatible. Exposes substrate access (bq_query, search_substrate, list_recaps, vm_read_file) and bounded action tools (ask_gemini, write_file, trigger_refresh, watcher_add_source). Same interface for every agent: human-in-the-loop chat, ADK-orchestrated agents, future Gemini Enterprise consumers.**Front-facing surfaces.** Multi-radar product layer at qld2032.com: Materials, Workforce, Disruption, Tenders, Pipeline. Contractor directory cross-matching 148K Queensland businesses across seven regulated sources. Live council DA tracking across 13 councils.**Track 2 entry.** Hardening this prototype for production-grade reliability — observable orchestration, freshness assertions via watcher cron, schema-drift detection, cost-disciplined cache invalidation. Built and operated by solo founder on a sub-$100/month GCP run rate.

QLD2032 is a System of Action for Queensland construction procurement intelligence — a vertical-specific implementation of the Agentic Data Cloud pattern Google announced at Cloud Next 2026, built independently by a solo founder.


Domain registered 2019, dormant 2021-2025, active build commenced November 2025.


The platform's first four months were built on a Samsung S23 Ultra phone between first-aid kit servicing jobs across Gold Coast and Sunshine Coast worksites: AI-generated code copy-pasted into WordPress custom HTML blocks and HFCM (Header Footer Code Manager) snippets, validated by a manual AI Council (Claude reasoning + Gemini grounding + GPT review + Perplexity fact-checking) where only consensus items shipped.


Track 2 — hardening this existing prototype for production-grade reliability.


### Problem to solve


Queensland's construction supply chain practitioners — estimators, project managers, tier-1 builders, subcontractors — operate across 35+ fragmented Queensland government data sources with no consolidated real-time intelligence layer. Procurement portals, council development pipelines, contractor licence registers, compliance databases, tender feeds, valuation data: each has its own schema, cadence, and silent failure mode (a recent example: a Queensland tender feed silently changed its status enum and froze data for 27 days before observation). Practitioners either build ad-hoc manual tracking or operate blind to upcoming procurement, contractor qualification status, and compliance changes.


### Our solution


QLD2032 is structured in four architectural layers, mapping directly to the Agentic Data Cloud pattern:


**Substrate brain.** A 113M-row BigQuery brain (11 GB across 64 tables) cross-referencing the full Australian Business Number register against Queensland-specific layers — 3.5M G-NAF addresses, 3.5M DCDB cadastral lots, 1.8M Valuer-General records, 530K historical procurement contracts, 196K QBCC-licensed contractors, and 9,824 first-seen council development applications across 13 councils. A Gemini File Search store grounds agents in a 78-file platform decision archive and (in active build) dark-data sources: regulatory PDFs, council DA documents, tender attachments, industry references.


**AI Council orchestration.** Multi-model agents specialised by what each is reliably good at — Claude Opus 4.7 for reasoning and synthesis, Gemini for grounding and spatial queries, Sonnet 4.6 for high-throughput drift detection. All agents query the same shared substrate via a uniform tool interface. Migration to ADK on Gemini Enterprise Agent Platform underway for Chef (substrate ingestion), Brief Writer v3 (citation-grounded procurement intelligence), and Watcher (drift detection). The manual phone-based AI Council that bootstrapped the platform now has a managed migration path on Google's just-released BYO-MCP and Model Garden infrastructure.


**MCP tool/action layer.** An OAuth 2.1-protected MCP server (hAPI) running as a hardened systemd service on a single GCP Compute Engine VM. BYO-MCP compatible. Exposes substrate access (bq_query, search_substrate, list_recaps, vm_read_file) and bounded action tools (ask_gemini, write_file, trigger_refresh, watcher_add_source). Same interface for every agent: human-in-the-loop chat, ADK-orchestrated agents, future Gemini Enterprise consumers.


**Front-facing surfaces.** Multi-radar product layer at qld2032.com: Materials, Workforce, Disruption, Tenders, Pipeline. Contractor directory cross-matching 148K Queensland businesses across seven regulated sources. Live council DA tracking across 13 councils.


**Track 2 entry.** Hardening this prototype for production-grade reliability — observable orchestration, freshness assertions via watcher cron, schema-drift detection, cost-disciplined cache invalidation. Built and operated by solo founder on a sub-$100/month GCP run rate.

beacn.space

I'll be writing this by hand, fast and loose, because I think that kind of thing is appreciated nowadays.

Problem to solve

What's good? Really. What is it?

That's the problem beacn.space sets out to solve.

The answer to this question has never been more important or interesting. For humans and for AI.

Our solution

beacn.space is a museum for artists and everyone to share their visions of goodness.

It's an app where we can scroll through inspiring content.

It's a platform where we can vote to democratically decide what we, as a species, want for the future.

It's a factory that produces more and more, newer and newer, better and better visions of the future.

These visions are a guiding light for humanity. A beacon.

AND IMPORTANTLY:

These visions are a guiding light for AI. And here's how:

beacn.space takes every post and embeds it into a high-dimensional, multimodal vector space, using Google's natively multimodal embedding model, Gemini Embedding 2 (without which this wouldn't be possible). We then take an average of those vectors, weighted by the number of votes they receive, to derive the "goodness vector," aka the "beacn of goodness." This is the novel idea that opens up a whole new field of research in alignment labs around the world.


There's a whole lot more to it, but this is a good start!
